racecards.race_name: racingtv.com Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Nice pedigree but triple-figure prices and well beaten in two 6f events; handicaps soon
runners.spotlight: Useful sprinting pedigree but he's gone off at triple-figure prices for both 6f starts and been beaten about 10l each time; drops to 5f with plenty to find and will be bearing down on ordinary handicaps soon.

runners.comment: Big prices and always behind in two 6f novices at Thirsk; handicaps will offer more scope
runners.spotlight: Big prices and always behind in two 6f novices at Thirsk; modest handicaps will offer more scope in due course.

runners.comment: Beaten favourite last 3 starts but still shown useful form in all his 5f races; solid
runners.spotlight: Can lead; beaten favourite on last three outings but he's run to a useful level in all his 5f starts on good and good to firm ground (in cheekpieces latest, retained) and has strong form claims again today.

runners.comment: Minor promise on the face of it at 5f and 6f but looks capable of better at this level
runners.spotlight: Minor promise when fifth of nine on Lingfield debut (5f) in June and at 14-1 he wasn't one of the outsiders for a valuable sales race at Newmarket (6f) last month, never near the leaders but still looked to have some potential; may well fare a lot better in an easier race.

runners.owner: Mrs P A Johnson & C H Stephenson
runners.comment: 20-1 for 6f debut here, ran fast until fading late; this trip might suit better for now
runners.spotlight: Out of a speedy mare and this one showed some dash himself before fading on his 6f debut here (20-1) last month; should come on for the run and may well find 5f suits better; can do better.

runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
runners.comment: Precocious pedigree but ran green and never seen with a chance on Carlisle debut (6f)
runners.spotlight: Has a fairly precocious pedigree but he ran green on his Carlisle debut (6f; 12-1) and was never seen with a chance; needs a sizeable step forward to have any chance and handicaps might already be the aim.

runners.comment: Promising 5f debut; pulled hard and onepaced 4th over 6f since; return to 5f can suit
runners.spotlight: Promising start when runner-up to a much more experienced rival at Beverley (5f, good to firm), keeping on nicely, but he pulled hard when upped to 6f at Hamilton last month and others found more late on; the return to 5f can suit and he shouldn't be far away.

runners.comment: Some promise behind useful rivals in two 5f starts but improvement needed
runners.spotlight: The form of her debut fourth of seven at York (5f, good to firm; 20-1) has worked out and she might have found the stiff finish at Hamilton against her next time, albeit behind a useful rival; might be better off on this track but improvement needed.

racecards.verdict: Although expensive to follow SCHRODINGER'S CAT doesn't lack for consistency or heart and this could be the right race for him at last.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/7 Schrodinger's Cat, 5/1 Enter Sandman,  Star Noir, 7/1 Regal Dream, 20/1 I'm Dan Dare, 25/1 Watchdog, 66/1 Hares Bredth, 100/1 Hey Havana

racecards.race_name: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Selling Stakes

runners.comment: Reliable colt who should appreciate the return to 6f; each-way shout once again
runners.spotlight: Thoroughly exposed but he is a reliable sort and comes here on the back of two good nursery efforts (5f); returning to 6f in his favour; just held by Carnation Queen on their York run in June but still high on the list.

runners.comment: Beat U Turn in a York seller in June; well held on soft last time; this is more suitable
runners.spotlight: Got the better of U Turn in a York seller in June (6f, good to firm; same terms); flopped on soft ground (7f) on her nursery debut at Catterick eight weeks ago but should be seen to better effect this time.

quotes.race: Coopers Marquees Rous Selling Stakes
quotes.quote: It was a bit of a surprise. Carnation Queen's last run was a big improvement from the first day, and from halfway I was confident and she was very tough. She was in the headlights a bit on her first day, but she's come on a lot - Harry Russell, jockey.

runners.comment: Banging at the door before Friday's Chester win (6f, good); strong claims dropped in class
runners.spotlight: Model of consistency this year, finishing second four times in succession before breaking her duck at Chester on Friday (6f, good);  solid claims dropped into selling company.

runners.comment: AW win on debut; mixed bag since; good weights chance but well below par latest
runners.spotlight: Won a three-runner Southwell fillies' maiden on debut (5f) in April; hasn't kicked on from that since, including in selling company, and although she has a good weights chance, she does need to bounce back from a poor run at Newcastle 18 days ago.

runners.comment: C&D winner in May; similar form in defeat since; each-way claims
runners.spotlight: Made all in a C&D maiden in May (good to firm), her second start; several comparable efforts in defeat since, most recently a second of eight in a Class 6 nursery at Beverley 17 days ago (7.4f, good to firm); place claims once again.

runners.comment: 40-1 and well held on last week's Newcastle debut (5f); a big step forward is needed
runners.spotlight: 40-1 for last week's Newcastle debut (5f) and ran to just a modest level; others are stronger.

runners.comment: Tough task at Doncaster on Friday; earlier maiden form brings him right into the reckoning
runners.spotlight: Stiff task in a Doncaster nursery on Friday and finished down the field; he had earlier finished third in three sprint maidens and he's one to take seriously dropped in class; stable also run U Turn.

runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
runners.comment: Ran green and dropped away from 2f out on recent debut (7f); down in class but needs more
runners.spotlight: 33-1 for his Haydock debut (7f, good) 12 days ago, a race where he ran green and weakened from 2f out; drops in grade with improvement possible but others bring more pressing claims.

runners.breeder: D Shapiro, B Haslam, J & J Feeney
runners.comment: First foal of a 6f/7f AW winner (RPR 70); yard not renowned for winning with 2yo newcomers
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 6f/7f AW winner (RPR 70), herself a half-sister to three winners; starts off in selling company and her stable is not renowned for winning 2yo newcomers.

runners.comment: Promising debut fourth hasn't been backed up in two subsequent runs; yard run two here
runners.spotlight: Beaten just over 1l into fourth on her Windsor debut in July (6f, good) but two lesser efforts have followed; drops in class but with improvement required; connections also run Social Exclusion.

runners.comment: Two runs in May, failing to build on debut effort 16 days later (C&D); improvement a must
runners.spotlight: Two runs in May, midfield at Thirsk (5f, good) on debut before a heavy defeat over C&D (good to firm) 16 days later; needs plenty more back from a break.

racecards.verdict: A competitive seller but MOSSVALE DIVA gained an overdue first win on Friday and can follow up at the main expense of U Turn.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Mossvale Diva, 9/2 U Turn, 6/1 Social Exclusion,  Dream More, 7/1 Willowinghurn, 8/1 Carnation Queen,  Showcasis Oasis, 10/1 Spirit Of Mara, 33/1 Archangel Josepi,  Charcon,  Sister Lizzie

racecards.race_name: In Memory Of Peter & Shirley Fryett Handicap

runners.comment: Three wins in 2024 but not in such good form this time; well treated but a bit to prove
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year, over 5f/6f on going varying from good to firm to soft; hasn't been in the same form this season, although fair run when second to a progressive sort at Yarmouth two runs back (5f, good; favourite); not so good last time; 5lb below last winning mark; bit to prove.

runners.comment: 0-8; well beaten on all three starts for this yard this term and others stronger
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; well beaten on all three starts for this yard this season, last time finishing over 17l last of six at Yarmouth when tried in headgear (5f, good); no headgear this time; others look stronger.

runners.comment: Best on fast ground; Pontefract winner in June and placed twice since; each-way chance
runners.spotlight: Mainly consistent form this term, winning at Pontefract in June (5f, good to firm) and finishing in the places twice since; best on fast ground; each-way chance.

quotes.race: Pontefract Ladies Day Wednesday 6th August Handicap
quotes.quote: That was a bit of an easy watch. A little bit of a long last furlong but Match Play stuck to his task good and hit the line strongly. A stiff track might have been the making of him - Paul Midgely, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: I was delighted with him at Leicester, he came home well and will step up to six furlongs at York. 10-05-22

He's been busy and thrives on his racing. The ground couldn't be fast enough for him, so he's had his conditions this year. He's been consistent, even though he's won only once. The draw counted against him in the jump jockeys' race at York. 14-08-25

runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner
runners.comment: 6f winner on debut but has run mainly over 5f since; fair run latest; quite interesting
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Ripon novice on debut in April (good); has raced mainly at 5f since, fair run when 2.75l fifth of eight at Catterick last time (5f, good; hampered in closing stages); quite interesting.

runners.comment: 0-8; runner-up on last three starts, latest on AW at Lingfield; career high mark now
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; runner-up on her last three starts, latest a close second on the AW at Lingfield; on a career-high mark now but wouldn't rule out.

runners.comment: Two wins at Beverley in July and excuses for lesser run there last time; a possible
runners.spotlight: Two wins over 5f at Beverley in July (soft/good to firm; went up 8lb) but tailed off after being badly hampered there last time; beaten a neck over this C&D earlier in the season; a possible.

runners.owner: R R D Saunders And Partner
runners.comment: Took this race off 3lb higher in 2024; AW win (headgear switch) last week; good chance
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year, the second in this race off 3lb higher (good to firm); off the mark for the season when scoring over 5f on Newcastle AW last week (first-time visor replaced blinkers); should give another good account.

runners.comment: Only win was on soft ground but acts on good to firm; good run latest; interesting
runners.spotlight: Placed a couple of times on turf earlier this season, including over C&D off 5lb higher in May (good to firm); mixed form since (decent run on AW latest); only turf win was on soft last October so rain a bonus; interesting

runners.comment: Four wins, including two for today's rider, this term; feasibly treated; one with a chance
runners.spotlight: Four wins, including two for today's rider, this season, latest on AW; still feasibly treated; one with a chance.

runners.comment: Her wins have been on stiff tracks; fair run in first-time headgear latest; place chance
runners.spotlight: Has run well on occasions on flat tracks, but her three wins have been on stiff tracks (two at Beverley, one at Carlisle); creditable run in first-time cheekpieces (retained) last time; place chance.

quotes.race: Rob Burrow Dare To Dream Handicap
quotes.quote: Opal Storm likes the place but she just needs a bit of luck in running - Brian Rothwell, trainer.

runners.comment: Only win was off 6lb lower over C&D last month; excuses for poor run latest; interesting
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at ninth attempt when winning classified race off a 6lb lower mark over C&D last month (good to firm; second has won twice since); can be forgiven a poor run last time (slipped leaving the stalls); quite interesting.

runners.comment: Two wins in 2024, including over C&D; rather disappointing this term; bit to prove
runners.spotlight: Won twice last season, the second one over C&D off this mark on fast ground; good reappearance run this term but mainly disappointing since; bit to prove now.

stable_tour.quote: She won twice for us over 5f last season at Musselburgh and Redcar and was placed on other occasions. I fancied her to go close last time at Catterick but she reared up when the stalls opened and that cost her a bit of ground. She likes really good, fast ground. I'm hoping she'll be winning again before long. 21-05-25

runners.owner: Con Harrington And Caballo Racing Two
runners.comment: 6f AW winner for Clive Cox in 2024; below-par this term; well treated; others appeal more
runners.spotlight: 6f AW winner for Clive Cox in 2024; mainly below-par this season; well handicapped now; others appeal more.

racecards.verdict: Having come back to form to win at Newcastle on his latest start ZUFFOLO (nap) is taken to repeat last year's win in this race.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Money Pockets, 9/2 Zuffolo, 7/1 Fletcher's Flight,  Angelic Bugler,  Jeany May, 15/2 Match Play, 10/1 Irish Dancer, 11/1 Without Flaw, 12/1 Opal Storm, 14/1 Doralee, 18/1 Jamie's Choice, 33/1 Nad Alshiba Snow, 66/1 Crafty Spirit

racecards.race_name: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Now Handicap

runners.comment: 20,000gns buy in July; won twice over 5f last term; seems to stay 6f; stable debut
runners.spotlight: Ex-George Scott; won a 5f Ripon maiden (good to soft) and a 5f Beverley nursery last season (good to firm); bit below that form this season; bought for 20,000gns in July; having second run over 6f on stable debut (fair run on first attempt at the trip).

quotes.race: Dacre Arms Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Mission Command is very uncomplicated and Callum had him in the perfect position. When the other horse came to him, he looked to kick again. He's a lovely, fun horse for the remainder of the season. We'll probably have to stay away from softer ground, he has that real fast-ground action. Five furlongs looks his bag and he'll stay over that trip. He'll go up a few pounds but we'll go up in grade at some stage - George Scott, trainer.

runners.comment: Best over 7f/1m on the AW; has a bit to prove down in trip on her first run since April
runners.spotlight: Only turf win was in a four-runner handicap over 1m at Pontefract in September 2022; has won five times over 7f/1m since then; back after a break on only third run at 6f; other stronger.

stable_tour.quote: A 10,000gns purchase who's been a revelation this winter. She's gone from strength to strength. To contend for the Arc Horse of the Year prize you have to keep them busier than you would like, but she thrived on it. Her last two wins at Wolverhampton and Southwell were arguably her best two runs of the whole campaign and she was fourth overall. We'll bring her back on the turf when she's had a rest. It'll be interesting to see if she can continue her progress. 23-05-24

runners.comment: Has won two of his last four races, both over 6f; below-par latest; could bounce back
runners.spotlight: Has won two of his last four races, both over 6f, at Nottingham (good) and at Pontefract (good to firm); not at his best last time, but could bounce back.

stable_tour.quote: He's always looked like he'll stay further and he does get 7f, but he's a bit keen and you have to be able to switch him off within the first 100 yards and they don't go fast enough over 7f. Over 6f he can relax and his stamina kicks in during the last furlong. He relies on other horses setting the race up for him and it worked out perfectly for him at Pontefract last week - he settled, got there and did what he had to do. He's still potentially well handicapped. 14-08-25

runners.comment: Won over 5f and 6f in May; some fair form since, but below his best last twice
runners.spotlight: Won at Beverley (5f, good to firm) and York in May (6f, good); some fair form since but not at his best last twice and needs to bounce back.

quotes.race: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
quotes.quote: I'm chuffed to bits. The horses are just starting to run well and it might not have been the best of races, but a winner is a winner at York. Ingleby Archie\P toughed it out and Harrison is riding well. He's not the easiest as he can revved up. He completely blew out at Pontefract. I was sure the track would suit, but not walking across the Knavesmire. It was a 0-78, but the handicapper will definitely have a say as it was at York! - Adrian Nicholls, trainer.

quotes.race: JW 4x4 Northallerton Handicap
quotes.quote: Ingleby Archie was getting warm in the saddling boxes and while it was humid in there I admitted to the owner it was a bit of a worry. On last year's form he looks quite well handicapped but it took a couple of runs to get going - Adrain Nicholls, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He won last time out at Redcar - it took me long enough to do it. He's a grand horse who finished second three times in a row. He got beat at 1-8 at Southwell on his comeback in February, but I don't have them tuned up in the winter so much. Hopefully he can come forward from his win as his form last year, when he kept knocking into one, was good. 26-07-24

runners.comment: Off the mark on handicap debut at Haydock in July; poor run latest; likely improver
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on handicap debut when scoring at Haydock in July (6f, good; beat Lord Abama by 0.5l); below-par run after a break last time, but is an unexposed sort who could progress further.

quotes.race: Lancashire DAF Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Pal Joey is still a bit immature and inexperienced, but he really had to knuckle down at the end. He shouldn't be overly bothered by the handicapper and I thought out of the field he ought to improve the most. This track suited him, but he'll be a nice horse for the autumn as he likes to get his toe in a little bit. That's as quick as he'd want it - Ben Haslam, trainer.

runners.owner: Mrs K S Pratt, P R Blackett, M Dods
runners.comment: Two wins this term; good C&D second last time and one to consider.
runners.spotlight: Two wins this term, at Newcastle in March (6f) and at Thirsk in June (6f, good to firm); sound run when runner-up over C&D last time (good to firm); worth considering.

runners.comment: Four wins were on AW last year; good second on reappearance but disappointing since
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been over 6f on the AW; good second to Ingleby Archie (now 3lb worse off) at York on reappearance in May but rather disappointing since; may fare better after a break.

runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall
runners.comment: Off the mark on handicap debut in April; stays 6f; below his best last twice
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on handicap debut at Doncaster in April (5f, good to firm); some fair runs since, including a good second over 6f, but disappointing on last two starts.

stable_tour.quote: He won well on his first start of the year at Doncaster and has possibly been a touch disappointing since. Hamilton next time was a complicated track for a horse who doesn't have the best of actions, and then he ran over five furlongs at York and may need to step up to six furlongs on a Flat track. There's a race at Ayr coming up that I have in the back of my mind for him. 02-07-25

runners.breeder: J A Knox And Mrs M A Knox
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho
runners.comment: 6f win at Pontefract in 2024; below best form this year but 3rd last time; place chance
runners.spotlight: Won off a 5lb lower mark at Pontefract in August 2024 (6f, good to firm); mainly below that form this year, best run when close third at Carlisle last time (6f, good to soft); each-way chance.

stable_tour.quote: A good filly whio won well at Pontefract and she could go for the Marygate at York on Friday. 10-05-21

She was third last time at Carlisle behind Lorna- it was the first time this year she'd really had some juice in the ground. She likes some cut and this year has been frustrating for horses like her as it's been quick ground constantly. She didn't surprise us by bouncing back to form last week. She's a consistent filly who manages to win one or two a year and she'll get her head in front again sooner rather than later. She has a great bunch of owners, Ursa Major, who are very enthusiastic. 23-07-25

runners.comment: Two wins last season; mainly disappointing this term, but good 3rd in visor two runs back
runners.spotlight: Won a Wetherby maiden (5.5f, good) and a Ripon nursery last season (6f, good); mainly disappointing this term, although creditable third in first-time visor (on again) two runs ago.

quotes.race: Paula Budge Surprise Birthday Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Lesley's Boy did well to win because he didn't really handle the track. I was off the bridle for most of the way, it was probably his ability that got him through today. It's a hard track, there are plenty of undulations but it worked out okay for me. He's improving with racing and I hope he keeps going the right way - Oisin Orr, jockey.

runners.comment: Only win in 15 runs was at Newmarket last year; in fair form at present; each-way chance
runners.spotlight: Only win in 15 attempts came off 1lb higher at Newmarket in August last year (6f, good); fair efforts in a visor (on again) when in the frame on last two starts; has each-way claims.

runners.comment: Two sound runs last month, winning at Doncaster on first occasion; should go well again
runners.spotlight: Two sound runs last month, beating Infinity Blue by 1.5l (now 7lb worse off) when winning at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) before going down by a nose at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) last time; should go well again.

stable_tour.quote: I think he is a very nice colt with a bright future. His work had been encouraging this spring and we started him off over 7f late last month at Yarmouth in what looked a particularly hot maiden, with several of the big yards represented and many trainers liking their charges. He ran a cracker to be third behind New Science, where he was rather green but ran on well. It was the perfect start to his career as he will have learned plenty and mentally handled things well. There are a few 7f maidens around Royal Ascot time later this month and we will pick our spot to make him a winner and take it from there. He runs in the colours of owner Khalifa Dasmal, who is a great man to train for. His patronage is largely thanks to my former guv'nor David Simcock giving him a nod in my direction. David has been a huge help to me in my career and I am very hopeful that I have a potentially nice horse here for Mr Dasmal. 10-06-21

runners.comment: Won maiden for Karl Burke last year before 35,000gns sale; interesting on first run at 6f
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f Windsor maiden for Karl Burke in May 2024 (5f, good to firm); bought for 35,000gns last September and best run since when 3.5l fourth of five at Doncaster last time; interesting on first try at 6f.

runners.comment: Has won over 5f/6f on good/soft ground; good third two runs back; a possible
runners.spotlight: Has won over 5f/6f on good/soft; good third at Ayr two runs ago (6f, good to firm); below best dropped back to 5f latest; should fare better.

quotes.race: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Taygar is tricky. She goes down early but she goes when she wants to go! She's not easy at all but she's got ability and she's just been waiting for some soft ground. Things didn't really go to plan but once Danny [Tudhope] got out she dropped her head a bit and settled well but, because of that, he had to send her on earlier than he would have liked. To be fair to her, she kept going and she's done it well - Michael Dods, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: I haven't done too much with my 2yos but this filly actually won first time out for us back in April. I don't usually have many youngsters out at that time of the year but she was showing a lot of speed and won well. Her next run at Pontefract was a different story as she was too keen and gave herself no chance, while at Redcar last time out she encountered good to firm ground for the first time and although she ran well, I felt she would appreciate a little more cut. We will look to upping her in trip to 6f next and find some slower ground. 26-07-23

runners.comment: Three wins this term, last 2 at Ripon in May (6f); could bounce back after poor run latest
runners.spotlight: Three wins this season, over 5f at Catterick in April (good) and twice over 6f at Ripon in May (good); well beaten last time but could well bounce back after a three-month break.

racecards.verdict: The choice in an open handicap is PAL JOEY, who won on his handicap debut in July and is better than he showed at Carlisle last time.
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/1 Novello Lad,  Papa Cocktail, 13/2 Lord Abama, 8/1 Ingleby Archie,  Dothan,  May Blossom, 10/1 Infinity Blue, 12/1 Pal Joey,  Taygar, 14/1 Alfa Whiteburd, 16/1 Lesley's Boy,  Barmyblade, 20/1 Mission Command, 25/1 Enola Grey,  Storm Call

racecards.race_name: Marske Fabrication And Engineering Straight Mile Handicap (Straight Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.comment: In good form recently with two 1m wins and a sound third over 1m1f; should be thereabouts
runners.spotlight: Has been in good form recently, winning over 1m at Doncaster (good) and Thirsk (good to firm) before finishing a creditable third off this career-high mark over 1m1f at Carlisle last time (good; fourth has won since); should be thereabouts.

runners.comment: Last win was over 1m2f in June 2023; fair 4th over same C&D latest; drop to 1m a concern
runners.spotlight: Last win was off 4lb higher over 1m2f at Doncaster in June 2023; fair fourth over same C&D last time (good to firm); drop back in trip not sure to suit.

runners.breeder: Mrs J K Powell & Catridge Farm Stud
runners.comment: Tough veteran; two wins in 2025, and won this race in 2023; should go well again
runners.spotlight: Tough veteran; two wins this term, the latest at Carlisle last week (1m, good to soft); won this race off a 3lb higher mark two years ago (good) and should go well again, particularly if the ground eases.

quotes.race: Lady Jane Bethell Memorial Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
quotes.quote: I didn't think I'd won when I crossed the line, I had no idea we'd dead-heated. Poet's Dawn is double the age of Star Start [fellow dead-heater] and had to carry 9lb more [taking into account jockey claims]. He's rarely out of the top three and is a star in the yard - Emily Roberts, jockey.

quotes.race: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
quotes.quote: It felt like the leader was getting away but Poet's Dawn has a big heart and he dug deep. He's a grand horse to have in the yard, he goes on any ground and very versatile - David Allan, jockey.

quotes.race: Ice Co Supporting Macmillan Handicap
quotes.quote: Poet's Dawn slipped coming out the stalls at Beverley last time and couldn't get in the race. He loves a bit of cut in the ground and David said he seemed to go better left-handed than right - Tim Easterby, trainer.

quotes.race: Racing UK In Glorious HD Novice Auction Stakes
quotes.quote: The race that Poet's Dawn ran in at Redcar last time worked out quite well with several winners coming out of it, and this didn't look a particularly good race - Tim Easterby, trainer.

runners.comment: Mixed form this term, but interesting with cheekpieces back on; further rain a negative
runners.spotlight: Mixed form this season, best run when third at Newmarket in June; quite interesting with cheekpieces back on having run well on previous occasion he wore them; further rain a negative.

quotes.race: MansionBet #Betstival Handicap
quotes.quote: I wouldn't say Siam Fox liked the track on this ground as he hit every undulation, but he got away with it. I didn't want to take any chances as I think a mile is as short as he wants to go. I think a different track and further will bring out more improvement - Silvestre de Sousa, rider.

runners.breeder: Fernham Farm Ltd & Mr E Cantillon
runners.comment: Below par since 7f win in April; goes well on soft ground so rain a bonus; new headgear
runners.spotlight: Mainly below-par since winning at Carlisle on reappearance in May (7f, good to soft; tongue-tie first time); best form with some give in the ground so would be interesting if the going eases; blinkers now tried (pulled hard with a visor).

quotes.race: Start Your RacingTV Free Trial Now Handicap
quotes.quote: Freddy Tylicki bought Rwenearlytheredad and Declan [Carroll, trainer]'s always thought a lot of him. I said to Declan to sell him in February but I might change my mind. I think he could put up a sequence now - John Blackburn, owner, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.comment: Won three of last five races, all over 1m at Brighton; bit to find with Poet's Dawn
runners.spotlight: Has won three of his last five races, all over 1m on good/good to firm at Brighton; has a bit to find with Poet's Dawn on their form at Carlisle last week.

quotes.race: Star Sports Classified Stakes
quotes.quote: Last time Lunanova just got there on the line, so she's never hit the front a furlong out like she did today. She really had to go and win it, and she did it very comfortably - Jamie Insole, joint-trainer.

runners.comment: Best run to date when third at Doncaster last time; further progress likely; chance
runners.spotlight: Showed best form on second handicap start (also second run in a hood) when close third to a subsequent winner at Doncaster last time (1m, good to firm; sixth also won next time); unexposed sort who may improve further; chance.

runners.comment: Completed a hat-trick in this race in 2024; hasn't been in such good form this term
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick of wins in this race a year ago (good to firm; 2lb lower mark); not in such good form this season and probably best watched for now.

runners.comment: Five wins on AW; good second on 3rd turf run last time after starting slowly; interesting
runners.spotlight: Has done most of his racing on the AW, winning five times at 7f/1m; was having only his third turf run when a good second at Newmarket on latest start last month (1m, good to firm; wore today's combination of headgear for first time), keeping on well after starting slowly when his blindfold came off late; interesting.

runners.comment: Last win was at Thirsk in June 2024; good third on stable debut (June); place chance
runners.spotlight: Won a Thirsk handicap for Mark Walford in June 2024 (1m, good to firm); lightly raced since, but was a good third at Leicester on stable debut this year (1m, good to firm); lesser run on AW latest; place chance.

runners.comment: 2-27 on turf, last win over two years ago; some fair efforts since; others look stronger
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-27 on turf, his last win coming at Beverley in September 2023 (1m, good); has run a few fair races since wind surgery in September last year; others look stronger.

quotes.race: Chapter House Handicap
quotes.quote: Tina (Jackson, trainer) has done well with Park Street because he's not easy. They've gone a good, even gallop which has suited me and he finished off well - Graham Lee, jockey.

runners.comment: Off the mark over C&D last month; needs to bounce back from a disappointing run last time
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when beating a subsequent winner over C&D last month but needs to bounce back from a disappointing run at Musselburgh 18 days later (1m1f, good).

racecards.verdict: This can go to the unexposed RENEWABLE DREAM, who showed improved form when third in a decent race at Doncaster last month.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Jez Bomb, 100/30 Poet's Dawn, 11/2 Renewable Dream, 13/2 Just Typical, 15/2 Lunanova, 12/1 Captain Corelli,  Park Street, 14/1 Siam Fox, 16/1 Rwenearlytheredad,  Marry The Night, 22/1 Island Of Skye

racecards.race_name: Market Cross Jewellers Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Won on stable debut at Nottingham in May but diminishing returns in three runs since
runners.spotlight: Won at Nottingham (8.3f, good to firm) on stable debut in May but diminishing returns in three runs since and was only eighth of ten at Haydock last month; now back on last winning mark but he needs to rediscover his spark back up in trip.

quotes.race: Download The PricedUp App Handicap
quotes.quote: Padishakh has got great back form which suggested he was very well in here. He's been set some fairly tough asks but is one for major handicaps as the faster they go the better it suits him - Ed Bethell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: Had his first start for us in the Doncaster Mile when he didn't appreciate the heavy ground. He's got some good form in France and had a run in Britain at the end of last year for Roger Varian when he probably didn't handle Newmarket. From what we saw in France last year he possesses a good deal of ability and hopefully we can get him back on track. There's a lovely horse in there. 19-04-24

He was a really welcome addition to the team and won on his debut for us at Nottingham. I thought he'd be really progressive but he was drawn wide and dropped in at Ripon next time, which you can't do there. Then he didn't look comfortable throughout the race at Redcar so I'm fairly confident we can draw a line through that. We'll bring him back for another 0-90 mile handicap somewhere and hopefully get him back on the winning track. 02-07-25

runners.comment: In-form 6yo who looks worth a try at this new trip and he's in the mix
runners.spotlight: Won a big-field handicap at York (1m, good) in July and he's been beaten less than 3l at Haydock and York since; still 5lb higher than for that last win but this isn't as tough as last time and he looks worth a try at this new trip; possibilities.

quotes.race: bet365 Handicap
quotes.quote: Billyb was quite keen when we got him. He's settled down quite a lot and he's done that well - Craig Lidster, trainer.

runners.comment: On reduced mark but last win was in 2023 and has record of 887788 since last August
runners.spotlight: On reduced mark but his last win was two years ago and he has form figures of 887788 since last August; down the list.

quotes.race: Watch Racing Free Online At Coral Handicap
quotes.quote: Silent Film travelled much the best at Epsom and William thought more of a climbing track would suit him, like today. The plan was to be a bit handier, mindful of the drop back in trip. The pace was solid enough and once he got to the outside, he picked up nicely. He travelled much the best and I'm sure Charlie will go back up to the mile -  James Doyle, jockey.

runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd
runners.comment: Went close off reduced mark here last month and he's respected back up in trip
runners.spotlight: His last win was 14 months ago but he responded well to cheekpieces when going close off a reduced mark here (1m, good to firm) last month; kept on well in that race and he's respected off only 1lb higher on this step back up in trip.

stable_tour.quote: He ran disappointingly at Dundalk last time and he's been gelded now. He's rated 88 and he'll definitely win off that mark in January if we can find the right race. 05-12-24

runners.comment: His form has cooled in last two starts and he probably wouldn't want any rain
runners.spotlight: Beat 19 rivals when scoring at York (1m2f, good to firm) in June; had three close calls since then but things haven't gone well on his last two starts; still looks on a workable mark but has bit to prove and he probably wouldn't want any rain.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Dawn Of Liberation ran so badly at Newmarket last time and nobody could really understand that. He emptied, but was quite free, so a mile might be better for him. He's still a baby and I think Churchill's progeny will just get better as they go on. We thought a lot of him before Newmarket - Paul Roy, owner.

stable_tour.quote: He'd sometimes been running to 102 and other times to 82, so we've tidied his wind up and I think he'll go to a handicap at Newmarket on Guineas day. He wouldn't want it soft. 02-05-23

There's a £20,000 race at Musselburgh on Sunday lined up for this well-bred sort. It's for those rated 41-68 and, as he's 68, it's the perfect opportunity. This time last year he was rated 85, so he has become well handicapped. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Progressive 3yo who is 3-7 and she had a near-miss at Doncaster on Friday; strong claims
runners.spotlight: Progressive 3yo who made it 3-6 when scoring at Ayr (1m2f) last month and she had a near-miss in Class 2 at Doncaster (1m4f) on Friday; on same mark here and she's a strong contender on this big drop back in grade; her last run was on good to soft but her wins have come on good/good to firm.

quotes.race: Book The Ladbrokes Ayr Gold Cup Handicap (GBBplus Race)
quotes.quote: Ryka travelled okay. I don't think she was enjoying the ground that much, but she's got a nice attitude and gives her all. My head was down on the line so I had every chance - Danny Tudhope, rider, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: It's a nice starting place and we thought Ryka would run well, but she'll probably need further. She's done that really nicely, she's green as grass and will definitely come on a ton for it - Stewart Tosh, representing owners Akela Thoroughbreds.

stable_tour.quote: She won her debut on fast ground and then we pitched her into two stakes races on soft, but she didn't show up. We'll up her in trip on better ground as she has plenty of stamina in her family. She'll start off in handicaps with a view to going back into black-type races. 14-04-25

runners.comment: Reliable type who won over C&D (good) on his penultimate run; possibilities
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the ninth attempt when landing a C&D handicap (good; first-time cheekpieces) in July; held in fifth of 12 at Windsor last time but he was only beaten around 2l and that was a a creditable effort in a Racing League event; should go well again and he's in the mix.

runners.comment: Won two of his six handicaps and was runner-up at Haydock (1m2f) last time; shortlisted
runners.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; 3yo who has won two of his six handicaps, including when upped to 1m2f at Ayr on penultimate start; Haydock race 12 days ago did not pan out ideally and he probably had no chance with the winner anyway, but he beat the other 12; same mark today and he remains one to be interested in; yard also runs Ryka.

stable_tour.quote: A big scopey horse who made his debut last month. He tends to be a bit keen, so we rode him cold and he encountered traffic problems. He has the potential to win his maiden and be a decent horse later in the season. 14-04-25

racecards.verdict: David O'Meara has a strong hand with the progressive 3yos Obito and RYKA, with preference for the latter.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Ryka, 4/1 Obito, 11/2 Debora's Dream, 13/2 Billyb,  Bamburgh, 7/1 Padishakh, 14/1 Dawn Of Liberation, 16/1 Silent Film

racecards.race_name: Try Racing TV Free Now At racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap

runners.comment: Beverley win in May, and three sound runs since; below best over 1m2f latest; a possible
runners.spotlight: Below form when dropped to 1m2f last time; in decent form before that, winning at Beverley in May (1m4f, good to firm) and being placed on her next three starts, all over 1m4f; stamina to prove (below best only previous try at this trip) but a possible.

runners.comment: Two wins and a second on last three starts; worth considering despite being off since July
runners.spotlight: Sound runs on fast ground last three starts, winning over 1m4f at Leicester in May and over 2m1f at Bath in June before fair second at Newbury in July (1m5.5f); off since; worth considering

runners.comment: Winner over C&D last month; chance off 4lb higher providing the ground isn't soft
runners.spotlight: Gained second career win when scoring over C&D last time (good to firm); chance off this 4lb higher mark, providing the ground doesn't turn soft.

runners.comment: 0-8; sound run upped to 1m6f when second at Haydock last time; chance on that form
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; best run came last time on first try at this trip when runner-up to a subsequent easy winner at Haydock (good; made much of the running); chance on that form.

runners.comment: Unexposed; ordinary form including at this trip last time; down in grade; bit to prove
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; ordinary form so far, last time beaten 11l when ninth of ten in a Sandown handicap on first try at this trip (good; 66-1, squeezed out at the start); down in grade; a bit to prove.

runners.comment: Unexposed; ordinary form so far but improvement expected on first try at this sort of trip
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; mainly modest form, including in two handicaps over 1m2f last month; bred to stay well so improvement expected on first try at this sort of trip.

runners.comment: Off the mark in tongue-tie at Catterick last week and could go well again despite penalty
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at 14th attempt when beating the in-form course specialist Bollin Neil by a neck at Catterick last week (1m6f, good; pound out of the handicap, tongue-tie on for first time); could go well again.

runners.comment: Ex-Jack Channon; not shown a great deal so far and needs to improve on stable debut
runners.spotlight: Ex-Jack Channon; not shown a great deal so far and was beaten 26l when last of five on AW in May (2m); off since; needs to improve on debut for new yard.

racecards.verdict: In a competitive race for the grade APHRODITES ROCK is taken to get off the mark after her sound run at Haydock last time.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/8 Spectacular Style, 3/1 Aphrodites Rock, 7/2 Hiccups, 6/1 Spring Chorus, 16/1 Doddington Guy, 25/1 Stretch, 33/1 Division Day

racecards.race_name: Boots Opticians Uttoxeter Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.comment: Small-field C&D winner in June but too inconsistent to rely upon heavily
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 7yo who returned from a break to win three-runner C&D (good) contest in June but was tailed off here later that month; didn't run badly when well-held third at Stratford (2m6f, good) last time and won't mind if there's rain about but others have more persuasive profiles.

quotes.race: Happy 60th Birthday Nigel Kirby Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Roger Pol is an absolute dude of a horse. He won a couple of bumpers and was a very good novice hurdler. We went chasing last season with him and he won first time out, but he's not the biggest and he found it a little bit like hard work - Jamie Snowden, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Get The Best Odds At Oddschecker Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Roger Pol has won two bumpers now and the plan would be to put him away now for a hurdling campaign in the autumn. He's not very big but makes up in heart what he lacks in stature - Jamie Snowden, trainer.

quotes.race: Cazoo Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Roger Pol is a lovely horse and we trained his brother Adrrastos who won seven races. What he lacks in size he makes up for in heart and ability. He jumps like an absolute bunny and should have a really nice future - Jamie Snowden, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He won on his chasing debut at Southwell last month when jumping neatly and accurately. He can continue to progress over fences. 06-11-24

He's lovely, a really cool horse who won two bumpers and was third with a penalty in one as well. He's a cracking jumper and one to look forward to for novice hurdles, but he's far from my only hope in that division. He's got to be exciting. 28-09-23

runners.comment: Safely held in July, after a long layoff, but now only 2lb above last winning mark
runners.spotlight: Safely held over 2m4f here in July, after a 388-day layoff, but now only 2lb higher than when winning at Stratford (2m6f, good to firm) last summer; seems versatile regarding ground; could have a say.

quotes.race: Nally Bros Hyundai Supporting Longford GAA 3-Y-O Hurdle
quotes.quote: He was tough and professional. It's nice to win two races with him before the big guns come out at Leopardstown over Christmas. He doesn't mind that ground. I'd imagine his next start will come at Doncaster next month in a Grade 2 juvenile hurdle [bet365 Summit Juvenile Hurdle on December 14] - John Queally, trainer of Sacchoandvanzetti

runners.comment: Ran well in two recent chases; on handy mark for first hurdle start since 2023
runners.spotlight: Seemingly perked up by change of headgear when running well in two recent Worcester chases (albeit fell on latter occasion); on handy mark for this first hurdling start since 2023 and soft ground won't both him; player if the blinkers continue to keep him focused.

quotes.race: Sky Sports Racing HD Virgin 535 Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: That was very straightforward. Dr Newland was quite keen to keep it simple, there was no pace so Tiger Orchid could go forward and do it himself - thankfully he's kept his head in front - Sam Twiston-Davies, jockey.

runners.breeder: S C E A De Maulepaire
runners.comment: Class 2 C&D winner last summer but this veteran has been out of form this year
runners.spotlight: Veteran who gained his latest win in Class 2 C&D contest (good; has also won on heavy) last summer and is now 9lb below that mark; out of form this year, though, and needs to prove he still has what it takes.

quotes.race: Geary's Garage Ltd John Deere Main Dealers Rated Chase
quotes.quote: It was a great pot to win. I was toying with the idea of running him in the Munster National, he had a nice weight in it, but I was concerned about the ground. We couldn't leave this race behind us. He might go to Cheltenham in two week's time, he won there last year, and we'll give him a break afterwards to bring him back for those handicap chases next year - Gordon Elliott, trainer of Dinons

quotes.race: Barrow Novice Chase
quotes.quote: He's a good horse and he knows how to win. He doesn't ever do anything fancy but he keeps on winning. He's a nice horse to have. Davy said he probably should have let him stride on but Victoria Bay probably isn't a bad mare of Colin Bowe's either but all he can do is win. We could even step him up to three miles and that could even suit him better - Gordon Elliott, trainer

quotes.race: BetVictor Novice Chase
quotes.quote: Dinons is a big horse and was always going to be better over a fence. He was brilliant and you couldn't fault him - Jack Kennedy, rider.

quotes.race: Randox Health Novices' Hurdle
quotes.quote: Dinons is a big, honest galloping horse. He is a chaser in the making. I wouldn't be surprised to see him back over here again for a Graded three-mile race. He gallops and stays and if his jumping gets slicker he could be a very nice horse. He has won five races this summer and don't owe us too much - Gordon Elliott, trainer.

quotes.race: Edinburgh Gin 2018 Leading Horsemen Awards Novices' Hurdle
quotes.quote: Dinons did it well again and will go for a staying novice race at Cheltenham next month - Gordon Elliott, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Novice Hurdle
quotes.quote: He has improved for a jump compared to his bumper form and Gordon will find plenty more races for him to win -  Davy Russell, rider

quotes.race: Paul Byron Shoes Maiden Hurdle
quotes.quote: He was unlucky last time when he looked to have it in the bag and fell. He won't have too much more this year and I'd imagine we'll try to find a little hurdle race for him somewhere and he'll go chasing next year. He's a staying type, a big good looking horse who'll need plenty of time. I'd say he'd go on any sort of ground - Gordon Elliott, trainer of Dinons

runners.comment: Ran well in first-time cheekpieces last month but forecast rain dents appeal here
runners.spotlight: 4-20 over hurdles, with all the wins on good ground; close third in first-time cheekpieces at Market Rasen (2m7f, good; several flights omitted) last month and still unexposed over staying trips but forecast rain dents his appeal.

runners.comment: Ran big race in defeat over C&D in July and subsequent 3lb rise looks manageable
runners.spotlight: Did not progress after winning 2m3f maiden in spring 2024 but ran big race in defeat over C&D (good) in July and is probably still well treated after subsequent 3lb rise; raced only on good/good to soft.

runners.comment: Three Newton Abbot wins this season; held here since; forecast rain a worry
runners.spotlight: Progressive 5yo who won three in a row at Newton Abbot in June/July (2m5f-3m2f, good); found things a bit tougher when upped in grade over C&D last time, though, and the ground may turn against him today.

stable_tour.quote: He was unlucky not to win his only Irish point-to-point and we bought him shortly after at the Goffs Punchestown Sale. He disappointed on his Chepstow bumper debut last month, but he's not your typical bumper horse in that he's a staying type who'll need a trip now he goes over hurdles. 04-11-24

runners.comment: Absent since turned over at skinny odds in May but handicap mark ought to be workable
runners.spotlight: Cantered to wide-margin win in modest Hexham maiden (2m7f, good) in May, his seventh hurdling start; turned over at skinny odds in 2m4f novice there later that month and not seen again since but current handicap mark ought to be workable; raced mainly on good ground but once ran well on soft.

runners.breeder: Kenneth L Ramsey & Sarah K Ramsey
runners.comment: Won weak maiden in July; safely held in two subsequent novice hurdles; headgear refitted
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat in his prime but has modest 2-42 strike-rate under all codes, and his small-field hurdle win in July came in an uncompetitive race (main market rival disappointed); safely held in two subsequent novice hurdles for Tony Carroll and now back in a handicap with tongue-tie/cheekpieces refitted for stable debut; probably best on good ground.

stable_tour.quote: We were pleased with his run in the Ballysax, which was his first run on turf, and he will have learned a lot from that. He seems to have come out of that race well and he'll probably go for another Derby trial. 19-04-21

runners.breeder: K A Bartlett & J M Beever
runners.comment: Won off much reduced mark on Flat last month; first hurdle run since summer 2023
runners.spotlight: Won small-field Flat marathon off much reduced mark last month but has not run over hurdles since summer 2023 and his current ability in this sphere is hard to gauge; best suited by good or faster ground.

quotes.race: Sky Bet UK's No.1 Betting App Handicap
quotes.quote: I wasn't worried about running Red Force One again quickly as he's as fresh as paint - Phil Kirby, trainer.

quotes.race: Betway Summer Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: It wasn't the original plan to be that far back but they went a strong pace early on. Red Force One stayed on well and will jump a fence - Harry Cobden, jockey.

quotes.race: Wellchild Handicap
quotes.quote: I think the headgear has done Red Force One good, and the mile and a quarter helps too. I was a bit worried about the ground but he battled well on it. The leader went a nice pace and I kept him in my sights - Martin Harley, jockey.

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late John Wade

runners.comment: Looked back in prime form when placed at Sedgefield this month; likely contender
runners.spotlight: Completed quick hat-trick last November (2m7f-3m1f, good/good to soft; bumper winner on soft) and looked back in prime form when third at Sedgefield (3m3f, good) this month; a likely contender.

stable_tour.quote: A bumper winner on her debut last season, she hasn't been the most fluent jumper over hurdles and we've done plenty of work on her schooling. I thought she'd won at Sedgefield in November when she came there absolutely swinging before falling two out. She needed a bit of physio and she's improved for it, and when the ground comes good we'll look to run her over two and a half miles-plus. She's qualified for the Go North mares' final and could be one for that. 24-01-24

runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing & Robert Waley-Cohen
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time, after a break, and seems best on sharp right-handed courses
runners.spotlight: Always behind at Market Rasen last month, after a break; she's better than that but all her best form is on sharp right-handed tracks, and she probably doesn't want significant rain; sports new cheekpieces.

racecards.verdict: This might go to YOU SOME GIRL, who returned to form with a good effort at Sedgefield 12 days ago. Gwash is feared most.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Keel Strand, 4/1 Gwash, 7/1 Tiger Orchid,  Galata Bridge, 8/1 You Some Girl, 9/1 Pike Road, 12/1 Isle Of Sark,  Of Course You Can, 14/1 Roger Pol,  Dinons, 20/1 Sacchoandvanzetti, 25/1 Red Force One

racecards.race_name: Gem Property Solutions Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Well beaten on rules debut in June; upped from 2m with tongue-tie added here
runners.spotlight: Held every chance before penultimate-fence fall in an Irish point in spring 2024 but was well beaten on rules debut at Worcester (2m, good) in June; upped in trip with tongue-tie added here.

runners.comment: Placed in 2m novice here in June and today's move back up in trip ought to suit
runners.spotlight: Chased home now-useful rival in an Irish point in April 2024 (good to yielding) and was placed for first time under rules when third here (2m, good) three months ago; needs to improve again today but the move back up in trip ought to suit; considered each-way.

runners.breeder: S C E A Haras Du Ma & Mr P Ehrmann
runners.comment: 0-9 in points and absent since March 2023; rules debut today
runners.spotlight: 0-9 in points and absent since March 2023; could be considered only if attracting good market support before rules debut; tongue-tie added.

prev_owners.owner: John Johnstone, Capt Adrian Pratt & Lord Clinton

runners.comment: Useful and very consistent Flat stayer; a danger to all if he takes to hurdling
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat racer; not quite as good as he was but has finished second five consecutive times this summer (1m6f-2m) and will be dangerous here if he takes well to hurdling at the first attempt; versatile ground-wise.

runners.comment: Fair sixth at Wexford in March; can feature here if ready to roll on British debut
runners.spotlight: 0-3 over hurdles in Ireland for Seamus Fahey but was very respectable sixth at Wexford (2m, good to yielding) in first-time cheekpieces in March; goes without headgear on this stable debut but has a part to play if ready to roll after six-month break.

runners.comment: Looked a shade unlucky when very close second a Newton Abbot last month; good chance
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two 2m2f maiden hurdles at Newton Abbot (good) since winning a bumper there (good to soft) in June; lost out only by a short head on most recent outing, having stumbled just after the final flight, and has obvious claims here.

runners.comment: Fifth in good-quality Flat novice in July; bought for 32,000gns soon afterwards
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 19l on both Flat starts for Karl Burke last year but outran 66-1 odds when fifth in good-quality Doncaster novice (1m4f, good to firm; RPR 77) in July; changed hands for 32,000gns soon afterwards; sports new hood and tongue-tie on stable/hurdle debut; market helpful.

runners.comment: Tailed off in Newton Abbot bumper on debut in June; now switched to hurdling
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in Newton Abbot bumper (good to soft) on debut in June; can only be watched on this hurdling debut.

runners.comment: Not beaten far on rules debut but that race was slowly run and he never looked dangerous
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when sixth of eight on rules debut here (2m, good) in July but that race was slowly run and he never looked dangerous.

runners.comment: In rear throughout when a 300-1 outsider for recent hurdling debut (2m)
runners.spotlight: In rear throughout when a 300-1 outsider for recent hurdling debut at Worcester (2m, good); upped in trip with tongue-tie added here.

runners.comment: Contender if judged on bumper/hurdle form in Ireland for Gordon Elliott in the spring
runners.spotlight: Placed in 15-runner Cork bumper (soft) for Gordon Elliott in April and was not beaten far on hurdling debut at Killarney (2m1f, good) in May; upped in trip for stable debut (same owner); can make his presence felt.

racecards.verdict: Percy Shelley was useful on the Flat but slight preference is for the proven jumping ability of THREE PIKES.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/10 Percy Shelley, 3/1 Three Pikes, 7/1 Thoughtsarethings,  Famous Shoes,  Woodview Lad, 16/1 Flawless Clarity, 25/1 Ontherunagain, 50/1 Master Muggins, 66/1 Click On The Link, 100/1 Gigilou, 200/1 The Kings Soldier

racecards.race_name: Pete Ball 60th Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Ran well on chase debut for Gordon Elliott; sold for 17,000gns after heavy fall in August
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles for Gordon Elliott in the spring (2m3f, yielding) and launched chasing career with good second at Kilbeggan (2m4f, good) in June; not quite in same form again since, though, and was sold for 17,000gns after last month's heavy fall; could be on tough mark for stable debut.

stable_tour.quote: This guy won a Kelso bumper for Rose Dobbin. We liked that performance and he's a nice looking young horse. He'll be out in a maiden hurdle soon, probably starting off over two miles. 24-10-23

runners.comment: Absent since pulled up in March but still looks interesting on stable debut; rain helpful
runners.spotlight: Absent since pulled up for James Moffatt in March but ran well in her other three chases last season (all 2m4f); can probably cope with today's drop back in trip and the forecast rain will be very much in her favour; dangerous to ignore on debut for Robbie Llewellyn.

quotes.race: Seasons Greetings From Hexham Bookmakers Mares' Novices' Hurdle (Herring Queen Series Qual') (GBB)
quotes.quote: Lunar Discovery didn't jump well the other day, but she jumped cleaner this time. She showed a blistering turn of foot when she won her first bumper. She obviously wants ease in the ground - Jimmy Moffatt, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's the best mare I've trained. She's a half-sister to Bingoo and we won two bumpers with her last season. She just failed to shoulder a double penalty at Carlisle last week, but she's come out of it bucking and kicking. She was unlucky not to win there as the winner leaned on her up the straight, but afterwards her Racing Post Rating went up another 3lb from her Listed second at Haydock in December. We're looking for soft ground but her target could be the Mares' Novices' Hurdle at Cheltenham and we might enter her in one of the handicaps as well. We'd ride her prominently and the stiff track at Cheltenham will suit her. If she lines up there, I'm sure she'll acquit herself really well. She's a big, powerful mare and I'm so looking forward to her going chasing. She's going to stay three miles no problem - she could be anything over fences. 14-02-24

runners.owner: Owners For Owners Duke Of Luckley
runners.comment: Made all in May, after a break, but three heavy defeats have followed; rain a negative
runners.spotlight: Completed hat-trick when making all to beat Harel Du Marais at Southwell (2m, good) in May, after a break, but has suffered three heavy defeats since; swaps visor for refitted cheekpieces; rain a likely negative.

quotes.race: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: It was a step up in class and Duke Of Luckley had a lot of weight - it was a big ask. He's a strong traveller and loves jumping. When he gets in a rhythm like that he's gaining a length over his fences. This was his main target for his first run back as he loves it at Southwell. As for future plans, we'll see what the handicapper does and take it from there - Martin Keighley, trainer.

quotes.race: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Duke Of Luckley loves it around Southwell and takes some stopping from the front there. James [Best] gets on well with him and you have to be brave on him. He'd been a frustrating second at Worcester the last twice, being collared late on having run his heart out, but Southwell suits him better and he's a consistent horse. - Martin Keighley, trainer.

runners.comment: Versatile and prolific 10yo; not at best this year and may need the ground to stay good
runners.spotlight: Versatile and prolific 10yo with 15 career wins under all codes; on a very tempting chase mark now but wasn't at best on either appearance this year (Flat/hurdles) and may need the ground to remain good here.

quotes.race: Watch Racing TV In HD Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: El Borracho backed up his last run and he's run another good race - Toby Wynne, jockey.

quotes.race: Bathwick Car & Van Hire Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: We have been waiting for nurseries for El  Borracho and he has got plenty of size and scope to go on from here. He was a bit green once hitting the front, but the other horse came back at him and that helped - Simon Dow, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been a star, winning 11 races for us. He's won once on the Flat, four over hurdles and six chases. He's tiny but he loves it and jumps amazingly. He'll have a break now and we'll get him ready to run in March. On quick ground going left-handed he can take a lot of catching. 01-11-23

One of a trio of horses who have won four times for us this season, in his case twice over hurdles and twice over fences in the autumn. He finished third to Filey Bay at Doncaster last week and will now have a winter break. He'll come back in the spring when we'll pick and choose our races carefully. He still has a few pounds in hand if we go down the hurdles route as he's rated 121, which is 13lb less than his chase rating. I expect there's more to come from this lovely, genuine horse. 07-12-22

runners.comment: Not at best on last couple of hurdling starts but reverts to chasing on a very good mark
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner who ran quite well on the first of his two chasing attempts in November; not at best on latest couple of hurdling starts but reverts to fences on a very good mark and rain won't bother him.

quotes.race: Shropshire Homes Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We really like Whizz Kid and he's worked quite well at home. We got him from the Arqana sale in France and he was the most expensive horse Foxtrot Racing have ever bought. There's a nice race at Sandown on bet365 Gold Cup day [2m novice championship final] or a valuable handicap at Aintree which we might look to go to. There's a few options and it'll be interesting to see what the handicapper does as he hasn't beaten much - Dan Abraham, racing manager for Foxtrot Racing (Owners).

runners.comment: Safely held last time but his second here in July was a good effort; headgear switched
runners.spotlight: Safely held at Bangor this month and now 0-5 for new stable this season but his second here (2m4f, good) in July was a good effort and he'll be favoured by the forecast rain; fine over 2m; big player if perked up by change of headgear.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes Fanzone "Your Team Your Game" Rated Novice Chase
quotes.quote: Aslukwoodhavit jumped really well. It was an ideal race for him despite his inexperience - Henry de Bromhead, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: A nice type who I'm looking forward to seeing in a bumper. You'll see him shortly. 09-11-20

runners.comment: Won twice on good ground in spring and is still in good form; rain may scupper his chance
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 6yo who won twice on good ground in the spring (2m3f/2m) and remains in good form; still on a workable mark but significant rain may scupper his chance today.

stable_tour.quote: We did not get him out until December but the benefit of a run for a youngster is huge and he ran a good race when a neck second at Chelmsford. He has run twice this year going down close each time and he will not be long in getting his head front. We will run him on the all-weather as he is not going to get the soft ground h e needs. I would think 1m6f will be his trip. 15-08-22

runners.owner: Awr Consultancy Ltd & The Folly Partners
runners.comment: 2-4 over fences this season; below form last time, though, and forecast rain a worry
runners.spotlight: 2-4 since switched to fences this season, the wins gained on good ground at Ffos Las (2m) and Bangor (2m4f); below form when last in action, though, and may not be favoured by the forecast rain here.

quotes.race: Merched Y Mor Mares' Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: It's not going to work [the Junior National Hunt Development Programme]. They've got the penalty structure wrong with those [horses], and Obsessedwithyou ended up in no-man's land for two years. But it's nice to see her back on track - Jamie Snowden, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She lost her way a bit last season in the mud, but she has more to give off her handicap mark on decent ground and will jump a fence in time. 06-11-24

She started in those new junior hurdle races last season and won twice, while also finishing fourth at Listed level and in a Cheltenham Grade 3. I think she's unexposed because it might be hard for the handicapper to get a grip on those races. She's matured and strengthened, and the Grade 2 EBF mares' race at Newbury next spring could be a target for her. 28-09-23

runners.comment: Scored at Market Rasen last month and is now 3-7 for this stable; respected
runners.spotlight: 3-7 for current stable since March, the latest win gained at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) last month; recent 3lb rise does not look excessive and he won't mind if the ground is much slower here; respected.

runners.breeder: Haras De L'Etoile & A S Gabrielle
runners.comment: Not at best on latest outing but won twice in good style beforehand; has more to offer
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing when last in action 106 days ago but looked progressive beforehand, winning quite stylishly at Warwick and Newton Abbot (both 2m, good); slow ground also suits; may still have more to offer.

quotes.race: Molly Ollys Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Gwennie May Jem was suited by a fast-run race and Tristan [Durrell] gave him a lovely ride. The loose horse looked to help him home as he wouldn't do a great deal in front - Dan Skelton, trainer.

racecards.verdict: The suggestion is ASLUKWOODHAVIT (nap), who is well handicapped if judged on his 2m4f second here in June and sports new headgear here.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Harel Du Marais, 5/1 Beny Nahar Road,  Obsessedwithyou, 11/2 Gwennie May Jem, 7/1 Aslukwoodhavit, 8/1 Lunar Discovery, 10/1 Whizz Kid, 12/1 St Cuthbert's Cave,  El Borracho, 20/1 Duke Of Luckley

racecards.race_name: quinnbet.com Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

runners.comment: Returns from break on workable mark but all best form is on right-handed tracks
runners.spotlight: Very useful handicap chaser who is best known for his exploits around the right-handed tracks at Kempton and Ascot; returns from 169-day break on a workable mark and won't mind if the ground turns soft but has to go left-handed today and his usual cheekpieces are missing.

quotes.race: New Bet-In-Race With Coral Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Flegmatik was brilliant and just tanked round with me. I think he enjoyed taking the mickey out of me and did it well - Tristran Durrell, jockey.

quotes.race: Coral Trophy Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: What can you say? We had a plan to pull that off all season. It was a terrible run at Sandown, I don't know why and that knocked our enthusiasm a bit for today. Flegmatik has run a great race. He clearly loves it around here but it is gutting to get beat. Fair play to the winner, he was off the bridle halfway down the back straight, but he has kept on - Dan Skelton, trainer.

quotes.race: Coral 'Fail-To-Finish' Free Bets Handicap Chase (Challenger Middle Distance Chase Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: There was a little bit of a question mark stepping back in trip, not necessarily in terms of ability, I just wondered whether there would be one following us round and outsprinting us from the last. Flegmatik is getting the hang of it. He's always been a nice horse but he's taken a lot of time. I'm going to completely leave him until the bet365 Gold Cup as I've always had it in my mind that one day he'll be an extreme stayer because of the way he jumps and travels in the early part of his races - Dan Skelton, trainer.

quotes.race: Watch Racing TV In Stunning HD Juvenile Hurdle
quotes.quote: Flegmatik is a lovely horse. We have our eye on him as a chaser so we won't go wild with him this season - Dan Skelton, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ran okay at Ascot last time but the handicapper's got him way too high. He's good right-handed and he'll be a force again when he's off the right rating. 12-11-24

runners.comment: Posted career-best performance at Cartmel last month and is also suited by this track
runners.spotlight: Ran well in two competitive 3m2f chases here (both on good) during the summer and posted career-best performance at Cartmel (3m1f, good) three weeks ago, making smooth progress from the rear before keeping on strongly to score by 6l; evidently in prime form but will have something to prove if the ground is slower than good to soft.

runners.comment: Just a respectable fourth here last time but has excellent 8-11 strike-rate over fences
runners.spotlight: Has excellent 8-11 strike-rate over fences; behind Olivers Travels when fourth here (3m2f, good) on latest outing in July but still ran pretty well, and may yet have a better performance in him; raced mainly on good ground but has won on soft.

quotes.race: Yeeehaaa.Bet Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: The handicapper's been fairly harsh on Village Master, but he's got away with it today. He didn't really handle the all-weather bend, I had to get him rolling off it and when I got there and he's just pricked his ears a little bit. He's only five, so hopefully there's a bit of improvement again out of him - James Bowen, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Enjoy The Racing App For Free Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: I trained Village Master's half-brother, Portrush Ted, who was a very good horse for me, and I liked his second point. We've just been hitting the crossbar with the amount of places and seconds we've had, so hopefully the tide has turned - Warren Greatrex, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He was second in a point-to-point and is a half-brother to Portrush Ted, who was a classy sort for me a few years ago. He'll be a three-mile chaser in time and ran a very nice race at Chepstow on Saturday. He seemed to handle what were atrocious conditions - heavy, drying ground - but he jumped great and probably paid the price trying to catch the front three before tiring. We learned plenty and it was a positive start. He's one to note. 13-12-23

runners.comment: Won over hurdles last month but the ground may turn against him here
runners.spotlight: Won a point and a chase in the spring (good to firm/good), and recently exploited his lower hurdle mark at Cartmel (2m6f, good); may not have much margin for error off current chase mark, though, and the ground may turn against him here.

quotes.race: Grace & Dotty Fedora's And Feathers Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Do No Wrong had run here once and won, but having Brian [Hughes] ride for an outfit like us is like having Matt Le Tissier rocking up for a non-league side ? it's just that bit of class - Justin Landy, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Useful at his best but has regressed and become very inconsistent; had wind op in July
runners.spotlight: Useful at his best but has regressed and become very inconsistent; returned from layoff with two disappointing efforts this season and needs subsequent wind op to enable a revival; raced mainly on good/good to soft ground.

quotes.race: Close Brothers Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Jack (Quinlan) said from flag drop Mole Court wouldn't drop the bridle. He was just too keen, we usually ride him handy but he was so enthusiastic. Being in this wonderful place has probably lit him up. He'd done a lot through the race it was a valiant effort, when he gets to the front he won't shoot clear but he's hard to get past too - Ben Pauling, trainer.

quotes.race: Celebrating The Life Of Matthew Field Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Mole Court used to be wayward and looking around a bit, but that's the most straightforward he's been. He was just travelling and jumping well everywhere, which was great to see - Ben Pauling, trainer, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: He's come alive for a fence and won his fourth race in a row at Cheltenham last month, a run that started back in June. Hopefully he's value for a bit more and he'll run in the amateur riders' race at Cheltenham next week. Good to soft ground would be all right for him, but he wouldn't want it to be testing. He'll have a break through the winter and come back for the spring. 10-11-23

runners.comment: Course winner who returns here after pretty good third at Worcester last month; considered
runners.spotlight: Thorough stayer who won by 15l here (3m2f, good) last autumn and returns after a pretty good third at Worcester (2m7f, good) last month; significant rain would leave him with something to prove (unraced on ground slower than good to soft under rules) but, otherwise, he's a contender.

quotes.race: Download Fitzdares For Free Race Streaming Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: AJP Kingdom has improved with every run. He loves that good ground and can hopefully go on improving - Connor Brace, jockey, talking to Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Portal Construction Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Category 3 Elimination) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Ajp Kingdom is a lovely horse. This was the plan and we got him in early and he's used his fitness there. He showed his ability and will go hurdling next - Sally Randell, assistant trainer.

runners.comment: Won his last four races for Tim Vaughan; makes stable debut after £60,000 sale
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Tim Vaughan with four consecutive wins, the latest a very dominant front-running performance at Cartmel (3m1f, good) in July; still unexposed at around 3m but, having changed hands for £60,000, has hefty 21lb hike and steep rise in grade to overcome on this stable debut.

quotes.race: DragonBet Sponsors Alan Johns Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Bumpy Evans wasn't too bad on the way to the start but he wasn't very nice during the race. I didn't think I was going to win the whole way around and then to his credit he's very game at the business end - Alan Johns, jockey.

runners.owner: Bedford & Noel & The Can't Say No
runners.comment: Two chase wins this year; dropped 1lb since Worcester third in July; e-w claims at least
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner this year (3m/2m7f, good); dropped 1lb since keeping on for very respectable third at Worcester (2m7f, good) in July and should cope if the ground softens here; each-way claims at least.

racecards.verdict: A few warrant consideration but the answer might be stout stayer FEVER DREAM, who is 2-5 since his wind op in March.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Bumpy Evans, 100/30 Olivers Travels, 11/2 Village Master, 8/1 Flegmatik,  Do No Wrong,  Fever Dream, 9/1 Ajp Kingdom, 10/1 Mole Court

racecards.race_name: Mercia Power 10 Year Celebration Handicap Hurdle

runners.comment: Got career back on track with comfortable win last week; respected under 7lb penalty
runners.spotlight: Lost his way after very promising novice campaign in 2023-24 but appreciated ease in grade and when winning comfortably in first-time cheekpieces at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last week and still looks well handicapped under 7lb penalty; also effective on good to soft; unraced on anything slower.

quotes.race: John Haine Memorial Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Goodwin fought back after the last and Freddie's 5lb claim helped. The second is a nice horse, but that weight we had helped. We might have another run this season, but I'm not too fussed, although he goes on better ground so I suppose I'll have to look at something - Chris Gordon, trainer.

runners.comment: Ended last season with two novice wins; makes handicap debut after five-month break
runners.spotlight: 2-4 over hurdles, having ended last season with two 2m3f good-ground wins against novice mares; contests much deeper race on this handicap debut and has fitness to prove after five-month break but is open to further improvement and won't mind if the ground turns soft.

stable_tour.quote: We might start her off in the Listed bumper on Paddy Power day at Cheltenham on November 16. She looks like she's improved from last season and she's going the right way. 06-11-24

runners.comment: Sound effort when second at Worcester last month but forecast rain a worry here
runners.spotlight: Posted sound effort when second at Worcester (2m4f, good) last month but has modest strike-rate in recent years and probably won't be favoured by the forecast rain here; others have more pressing claims.

stable_tour.quote: He has been a little unlucky not to win this year with three seconds, all at Lingfield. Last week he was a little disappointing but they crawled round and he needs a good pace, so when they quickened he was caught out. He will have a break now before coming back on the turf. I think he can win on the grass in fact he could be a better horse than on the all-weather. He seems versatile regarding trip, but I feel 1m4f in a true run race would be ideal for him, as when it is run slowly he gets a bit keen and is hard to settle. 29-03-21

runners.comment: Promising 2nd on chase debut (2m) yesterday; 2m4f will suit better; correct mark today
runners.spotlight: 2-6 during first season over hurdles; made a promising start to chase career when a staying-on second at Worcester (2m, good to soft; 8lb wrong) yesterday; this longer trip will suit if turned out quickly off her correct mark.

quotes.race: racingtv.com/freetrial Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Springs A Girl should've never been that price. We bought her after winning a bumper in Ireland and really liked her run at Cheltenham, when she stayed up the hill strongly to be midfield. My horses were sick when she ran at Catterick so we put a line through that. She hit the line strongly and there's more to come - Martin Keighley, trainer.

runners.owner: Mrs C Clark, D Mullen & Partners

prev_owners.owner: Mrs C Clark, D Mullen & Mrs E Quinlan

runners.comment: Well handicapped on summer 2024 form, and may have needed reappearance run in July
