runners.spotlight: Started off for Lizzie Quinlan with three pretty good efforts last summer (one over C&D) and is versatile regarding ground; returned from layoff with quiet run in July but has possibilities here if that outing brought him on.

quotes.race: William Hill Epic Boost Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Everything seemed to go wrong really, I wanted to be a lot handier but they ended up doing nearly a standing start so that went to pot before we got going. But Elogio travelled nicely, jumped well enough and really dug deep, although he was hanging a bit left and he's best going left handed - Cameron Iles, jockey.

quotes.race: Boylesports "Junior" National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Elogio was an acquisition for the Flat to start with and this looked a suitable opportunity to get him started. I was quite surprised. He doesn't show an awful lot at home in terms of speed, but he's always shown a great attitude. He's school over hurdles and will come on for the run. We'll work out a plan - Richard J Bandy, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's done nothing wrong. He won his bumper by ten lengths on his debut at Aintree last season and has since been very consistent over hurdles without winning. The handicapper's probably got his measure for the moment, but hopefully he'll take to fences in future. He has to go left-handed - when we tried him right-handed at Ascot last time it didn't work at all. There are plenty of options over Christmas and he could even go up to Newcastle on Saturday. 20-12-23

runners.comment: Quite stylish C&D winner in June but not seen to same effect on next two outings
runners.spotlight: Looked back in prime form when winning quite stylishly over C&D (good; also suited by soft) in June but was not seen to quite the same effect on next two outings and has become hard to predict.

quotes.race: Half Year Annual Membership Now Available Mares' Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Callin Baton Rouge will have a couple more runs then I'll probably put her away for the winter. She'll be a nice horse for novice handicap chases in the spring. Adam Pogson, joint winning trainer talking to Sky Racing.

runners.breeder: Exors Of The Late John Harrington
runners.comment: Not firing on all cylinders at end of last season and needs break to have done him good
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner on ground ranging from good to heavy; on a workable mark now but wasn't really firing on all cylinders at end of last season and needs 145-day break to have done him good.

quotes.race: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: We knew Woldspear was fit and well but he'd had so long [493 days] off the track and some of these horses take time to get going again. He ran well and was full of running at the end. I was pleased so hopefully he can come on for it and win more races - Oliver Boyden, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He won very well on his debut at Huntingdon. The form has worked out well and the runner-up, First Street, has won twice over hurdles. He'll start off in a maiden hurdle in two or three weeks. 21-10-21

runners.comment: Absent three months since 7l Stratford win in June but still well handicapped on old form
runners.spotlight: Came good for new stable in June, making all to score by 7l at Stratford (2m2f, good); absent for three months since and not sure to be suited by the forecast rain here but remains well handicapped on older form.

runners.comment: Dual winner at end of last season but recent efforts have been disappointing
runners.spotlight: Struck form with two wins towards end of last season, the latter off 4lb higher at Kempton (2m, good); also suited by 2m4f and slower ground but recent efforts have been disappointing and he needs another revival.

quotes.race: JTC Pizzalicious Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Thank You Blue jumped really well. He'll probably be better over a little bit further, I was just forcing him for the last half a mile. He was good over the last two and galloped to the line - Sean Quinlan, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: We managed to get this lovely big rangy horse out for one run last term in a Wincanton bumper, where he ran a smashing race in what looked like a very hot contest. That was over 1m7f, but he has plenty of stamina and we will try and get another bumper into him next, although he is still around six or seven weeks off his return. I look on him as a medium-term project as you will not really see him at his best until he is seven or eight years old. 09-11-22

runners.breeder: E A R L Haras D'Enki
runners.comment: 0-6 under rules in Ireland; interesting to see what Ben Pauling can do with him
runners.spotlight: Well held before final-flight fall on handicap debut at Kilbeggan in May and signed off in Ireland with 36l defeat on chase debut in June; that said, it will be interesting to see what Ben Pauling can do with him.

runners.comment: Struggled on both starts for new stable in the spring; has too much to prove
runners.spotlight: Won three times for Olly Murphy last autumn (2m3f-2m4f, good-soft) but left that stable after two below-par runs in February (hurdle/chase) and struggled on both starts for new trainer in the spring.

quotes.race: Trustatrader Apply Today Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Bread And Butter is thriving and Lewis [Saunders] gave him a lovely ride today. There's only one winning post on heavy ground, so there's no point being in front at the top of the home straight. The wind op has helped him and I think it's boosted his confidence as much as anything as he has come down the weights from where he was. The race did fall apart too - Olly Murphy, trainer.

quotes.race: www.roscommonracecourse.ie Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Bread And Butter got a lovely tow into the race and did it well. I thought the handicapper might have been a bit hard on him after the last day! There's a 0-123 over 2m5f at Galway that we might look at - Gavin Cromwell, trainer.

runners.comment: Found 2m on good ground too sharp last time; rain will aid his cause here
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 7yo who won over 2m3f on heavy ground in spring 2024; well beaten over fences on stable debut in June and found 2m on good ground too sharp when back over hurdles last month but rain will aid his cause here.

quotes.race: Locke Burger Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: \BKing Of Cong\P did it easy enough and really handled the conditions and the extra three furlongs. Terence's assistant Michael Browne said to jump him out in the first four and, if he was good enough, to take your chance. We didn't go too fast so it paid to be handy - Darragh Allen, rider, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Makes stable debut after 698-day absence; best watched
runners.spotlight: Won Southwell maiden (2m, good) for Hughie Morrison in February 2023 but signed off for him with 33l defeat in 2m5f handicap later that year and makes stable debut after 698-day absence; best watched.

quotes.race: Fakenham Maiden Open National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I was really worried that Dom Perry would be too green for this race today and he was, really. Let's be honest, that wasn't the strongest bumper that's ever been run but he won well. I think what he really needs is time, so we'll have to decide whether we give him another go in a bumper of go over hurdles next. - Laura Mongan, trainer

runners.comment: Ran well in Irish maiden hurdle in July; now back with original stable; hard to predict
runners.spotlight: Won beaten on both starts for the Greenall-Guerriero stable last season (hurdle/chase) but arguably posted career-best form when sixth in Kilbeggan maiden hurdle (2m3f, good to yielding) for Gordon Elliott in July; now back with his original stable; not an easy one to predict.

runners.comment: Versatile regarding trip/ground but has fitness to prove after six-month absence
runners.spotlight: Rewarded enterprising ride in amateur jockeys' race at Catterick (3m1f, good) last December and also ran well when second over 2m3f there (good to soft) in February but absent since March; not the percentage call.

quotes.race: AJA Membership Includes Insurance Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Go North Brindisi Breeze Qual')
quotes.quote: Shay [Halton] rides Be The Difference every day at home, so it was nice to give him a chance on him because he's not the easiest of rides. I told him not to be afraid to go on if they weren't going fast enough as he's a bit keen, but it turns out they've gifted him the race more or less. He wasn't going that fast - Phil Kirby, trainer.

runners.comment: Needs to improve to make winning handicap debut but the step up in trip ought to suit
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than minor promise in her three qualifying races, with best effort her sixth of 12 here (2m, good) two starts ago, but ought to be suited by the step up in trip on this handicap debut.

racecards.verdict: Goodwin got his career back on track last week but a speculative alternative to consider is ELOGIO, who has slipped to a good mark.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Goodwin, 9/2 Audacious Annie, 5/1 Jhentong Enki, 11/2 Juan Bermudez, 8/1 Oslo,  Callin Baton Rouge, 10/1 Springs A Girl, 16/1 Wolfspear, 20/1 Elogio,  Thank You Blue, 25/1 Streamsforth Lad,  Be The Difference,  Nanny Daisy, 33/1 King Of Cong, 50/1 Dom Perry, 100/1 Bread And Butter

racecards.race_name: QuinnBet Handicap Chase

runners.comment: Back from long absence with two good thirds over hurdles this season; chase debut today
runners.spotlight: Just 1-11 over hurdles but returned from long absence with two good thirds this season, the latter at Bangor (2m3f, good) 11 days ago; soft ground also suits; player if he takes well to fences.

runners.comment: Returned to form in new blinkers last month and is still on a good mark
runners.spotlight: Returned to form in new blinkers at Fontwell (2m5f, good to soft) last month, where he finished only fifth of seven but was right in the thick of things until fading after the last fence; still on a good mark; shortlisted.

runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles; third here in May but two lesser efforts followed; chase debut today
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles; stayed on for third here (2m7f, good) in May but two lesser efforts have followed; drops back in trip for chasing debut and needs to up his game.

runners.breeder: R D & Mrs J S Chugg

prev_owners.owner: Fintan J Quinn & Liam Breslin & Mrs Anne McMahon

runners.comment: Placed off today's mark on both starts for new stable this summer; rain helpful here
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who was placed off today's mark on both starts for new stable this summer (2m7f/2m5f, good); any underfoot ease today will probably suit him, and he features prominently in calculations.

runners.comment: 0-13 over hurdles; second three times on good going in spring; rain a worry on chase debut
runners.spotlight: 0-13 over hurdles; runner-up three consecutive times on good ground in the spring but disappointed at Stratford two months ago and the forecast rain is a worry before this chasing debut.

prev_owners.owner: J Bedi, P Ellerby And Partners

runners.comment: Two convincing hurdle wins for new yard; strong claims if reverting to fences here
runners.spotlight: Evidently revitalised by switch to current stable when sauntering clear to win by 20l over hurdles at Fontwell last week (2m5f, good; has also won on soft); cosily landed the odds at Worcester yesterday and would have strong claims if he reverts to chasing here.

quotes.race: Kilmanahan Beginners Chase
quotes.quote: Shantou Lucky ran very well at Leopardstown last time and he is taking to fences. He will stay on the go probably but he might not run on good ground - Gavin Cromwell, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well enough at Fairyhouse this month. He'll mix and match between hurdles and fences and hopefully pay his way. 27-10-22

runners.comment: Dual chase winner in early part of this year but absent since pulled up in March
runners.spotlight: Did well over fences in early part of this year, winning at Catterick (2m3f, good to soft) and Doncaster (2m4f, good); absent since pulled up in March, though, and others appeal more this time.

runners.comment: Second on two of her last three outings but forecast rain not helpful here
runners.spotlight: Stuck to her task pretty well at Cartmel (2m5f, good) last month and has now finished second on two of her last three starts but the forecast rain is probably not helpful here; tongue-tie left off today.

runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles and needs to find something extra on chase debut; best on good ground
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles; stayed on for respectable fourth at Market Rasen (2m2f, good) last month and wasn't beaten far in first-time visor at Hexham (2m4f, good) a few days later but needs to find something extra on this chasing debut and will have something to prove if the ground is slower than good.

stable_tour.quote: A new recruit who ran in four bumpers for Mick Appleby, finishing third at Stratford on his most recent start in August 2022. He has settled in well and will be going novice hurdling. I'd imagine he'll be best on spring ground. 24-01-24

racecards.verdict: Shantou Lucky would be respected if turning out after yesterday's win but I AM ROCCO is the alternative selection.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/6 Shantou Lucky, 7/1 I Am Rocco, 8/1 Jipekaa Machin, 12/1 Rajjamataz,  Bravethewaves, 14/1 Let Me Go Boys,  My Virtue, 16/1 Getuptheyard, 25/1 Zuul

racecards.race_name: Download The QuinnBet App Handicap Hurdle

runners.comment: Two lesser efforts followed his promising hurdle debut but he's still open to improvement
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who made quite promising hurdle debut when fourth at Southwell (good to soft) in February; two lesser efforts followed, and this handicap debut comes after a 172-day break, but he he might be aided by the new tongue-tie and remains open to improvement.

runners.comment: Ran quite well in April, after a break, but well below that form since
runners.spotlight: 1-15 over hurdles; returned from break with fairly good fourth at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft) in April but all three subsequent runs were underwhelming and he has basically become disappointing.

runners.comment: Won four in a row last autumn but has performed well only once this year (four runs)
runners.spotlight: Won four in a row (two hurdles, two chases) at Sedgefield last autumn but has performed well in only one of four chases this year and needs to prove he can cope with a hurdle mark this high; best on good/good to soft.

stable_tour.quote: He's lively but is a great character. He didn't run as I anticipated in his bumper, but I'm confident he's considerably gifted and suspect we'll try to win a bumper before hurdling. 19-10-21

runners.comment: Ran well when third two starts ago; very patient tactics didn't work last time
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but ran well when third at Bangor (2m, good) in June and did not look suited by switch to very patient tactics when only sixth over C&D in July; also has form on soft ground; sports new tongue-tie; might be the answer.

runners.comment: Beaten favourite last time but was convincing C&D winner two runs ago; remains of interest
runners.spotlight: Seemingly aided by new tongue-tie for new stable this summer, most notably when coming from off the pace for convincing C&D (good) success; not quite as good at Stratford last time and not sure to be suited by rain but remains very much of interest.

runners.comment: Mixes hurdling with chasing nowadays and is inconsistent; has the form to feature, though
runners.spotlight: Mixes hurdling with chasing nowadays and is not very consistent but his fourth at Cartmel (2m1f, good) two starts ago was a pretty good effort and he'll cope if the ground turns soft here.

quotes.race: Breedon Handicap Chase (Go North One Man Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: I'd love to say I gave him a peach of a ride, but Restandbethankful ran away with me. I rode him in work at home and he did exactly that, but you don't mind it when they're winning - Tommy Dowson, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He ran okay on his first start at Chepstow when he lacked experience. He works like he's on a decent mark and that run will have stood him in good stead. He's capable of winning on nice ground over hurdles. 21-10-21

He was a good winner over 2m at Huntingdon last time in a handicap hurdle and that form has received quite a boost with the runner-up, Zacony Rebel, bolting up last week at Ludlow by 12 lengths. There is a good handicap in two weeks' time at Musselburgh's big meeting that is his likely next assignment and off a 6lb higher mark he should continue to be competitive. 26-01-22

runners.comment: Travelled well for a long way on recent stable debut; drops from 2m4f here
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles when based in Ireland but, following a ten-month layoff, travelled well for a long way on recent stable debut at Worcester (2m4f, good); needs to build on that effort but it will be interesting to see whether drop to 2m pays dividends; swaps cheekpieces for first-time hood.

runners.comment: Shaped well when fourth on handicap debut in March; a possible if tuned up after break
runners.spotlight: Fair on the Flat for Hughie Morrison; showed only minor promise on first three hurdling starts but shaped well when fourth on handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March; not out of this if tuned up after break.

runners.comment: Four-time course winner; well held in sixth at Worcester yesterday
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with hard-fought win here (2m4f, good) in May, his fifth hurdling success (three of the others were over C&D); well held back from a break at Worcester yesterday (about 5l behind Platenium).

runners.comment: Lurks on a good mark but recent chase/hurdle form has been dsappointing
runners.spotlight: Made disappointing start to chasing career this season (three attempts) and reverted to hurdling with a heavy defeat and a fall last month; lurks on a good mark but needs to turn a corner.

runners.breeder: E A R L Haras De Montaigu
runners.comment: Two promising runs for new yard, most recently a close 4th at Worcester yesterday
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after nine hurdling starts but bettered low-key stable debut when keeping on steadily from the rear for fifth at Market Rasen and he finished a close fourth at Worcester (2m4f, good to soft) yesterday; may have more to offer.

runners.comment: Ran well to a point here (2m4f) in June but tailed off when recently dropped back to 2m
runners.spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles; ran well to a point when fourth here (2m4f, good) in June, his second start for Christian Williams, but was tailed off when dropped back to 2m at Stratford ten days ago.

runners.comment: Has very mixed record since C&D since in May and is now 0-20 over hurdles
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with good second over C&D (good) in May but her subsequent record is very mixed and she's now 0-20 over hurdles; tried in new cheekpieces today; would probably prefer the rain to stay away.

runners.comment: Still unexposed over hurdles but needs wind op to enable significant improvement
runners.spotlight: Pulled up when upped to 2m4f for handicap debut in March and safely held when dropped back to 2m the following month; had wind op during five-month break and remains unexposed but significant improvement is required.

runners.comment: Placed twice off today's mark at start of this year but absent since a fall in March
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles; placed twice off today's mark at start of this year (1m7f/2m, good to soft) but absent since a fall in March; tongue-tie fitted for first time here.

racecards.verdict: Patient tactics did not pay off for PORTCAMMON here last time and he's better judged on his Bangor third two starts ago.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Diesel Line, 4/1 Spectre One, 7/1 Rock On Tommy, 8/1 Portcammon, 12/1 Restandbethankful,  Catboy, 14/1 Weir Lane, 16/1 Master Dancer,  Platenium,  Northern Rose,  Rhiannon Ring, 20/1 Clean Getaway,  Now Then Wendy,  Danny's Friend,  Hello Sweety

racecards.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Selling Stakes

runners.comment: Won two 6f novices here last autumn; might get away with new trip now dropped in class
runners.spotlight: Won two 6f novices here last autumn; his 3yo campaign hasn't been a progressive one but he shaped quite nicely in a Racing League race here (6f) on yard debut in July and can be forgiven a soft-ground defeat since; stamina query over new trip but he might get away with it at this modest level.

runners.comment: Won 1m novice on Polytrack as 2yo; this is easier than Goodwood seller she ran in latest
runners.spotlight: Untested on Tapeta but won 1m novice on Polytrack as 2yo; best handicap form this year when close third of five at Newmarket (1m2f) in August, despite hanging left, and she has a better chance in this seller than at Goodwood last time; back down in trip; good chance.

runners.comment: Very mixed so far but quite promising yard debut on turf (1m) and may yet do better
runners.spotlight: Promising debut on turf last August but he ran poorly on Tapeta next time and in two handicaps for previous trainer; showed more when a staying-on fourth of 12 at Windsor (1m) 15 days ago on yard debut and has a good run in him despite the unfavourable terms.

runners.comment: Well held in maidens but showed late promise over 1m at Beverley in June; down in class
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well held in two maidens but he did keep on quite nicely at Beverley (extended 1m) in June, when not far behind one who hacked up in a Goodwood seller next time (Nanny Park well held); of interest dropped to this level in first-time blinkers.

runners.comment: £25,000 yearling; nice pedigree but lack of experience could cost her
runners.spotlight: £25,000 yearling; third foal; by Coulsty; half-sister to 5f winner Scoops Ahoy (including AW; RPR 78); dam unraced sister to winners Al Qahwa (useful 6f including 2yo; RPR 103) and Caffe Macchiato (1m/9.7f; 89); lack of experience could cost her.

runners.comment: In the frame 4 times this year at 7f and 1m; very tough terms in this company
runners.spotlight: Has finished in the frame four times at 7f and 1m, on AW and turf, this year, but she has a very tough task on these terms.

runners.comment: Big prices and minor promise in Ireland; never sighted on yard debut; right down in class
runners.spotlight: Big prices and minor promise in Ireland and for new trainer, finishing way back in a Newmarket novice (1m) in August; this is a big drop in class so she could be much more prominent in first-time cheekpieces.

racecards.verdict: Nanny Park should go well but tentative preference is for GOOD BANTER who is suited by this surface and takes a drop in class.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/4 Nanny Park, 11/4 Good Banter, 5/1 Chelsea Old Church, 15/2 Babychino, 10/1 Self Charging, 16/1 The Doonan, 33/1 Freedom Bay

racecards.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com 'Confined' Restricted Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.comment: Held in a sales race after his winning debut, but bred to handle AW and could bounce back
runners.spotlight: Sent off 5-1 when making a winning debut at Bath (5.7f, firm) last month (runner-up won next time), but could never get involved when finishing in midfield in a sales race at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) a week later; half-brother to two AW winners and could easily bounce back.

runners.comment: Stable had a couple of 2yo debut winners in June; watch market
runners.spotlight: February foal by Ardad; 23,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 73), half-sister to winners Absolutely So (7f Group 3), Weekend Getaway (7.5f-8.7f) and Forbidden Land (7f), is granddaughter of a 1m2f Group 2 winner; stable only has the occasion 2yo go in first time, though had a couple do so in June; watch market.

runners.comment: Well held in two maidens; nurseries may be his scene after this
runners.spotlight: Well held in two maidens, the latest over C&D; nurseries may be his scene after this.

runners.comment: Showed promise on his debut in April, but poor on Beverley return; resurgence possible
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when third of four on his Musselburgh debut (5f, good) in April, but finished tailed off on last month's Beverley return (5f, good to firm) when not appearing to handle the track; dam won on the AW and no surprise to see a resurgence with Oisin Murphy booked.

runners.comment: Improved from his debut when a close third at Bath; a player if progressing again
runners.spotlight: Well held on last month's Chelmsford debut (6f, Polytrack), but much better when beaten less than a length into third of 11 at Bath (5.7, soft) 13 days ago; shouldn't have an issue with the return to the AW (dam won twice on it) and a player if progressing again.

runners.comment: Hinted at ability at Newmarket on his second start, but nurseries may be a better fit
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in both starts over 6f on good to firm ground last month, but hinted at ability at Newmarket on the second occasion; drawn widest and nurseries may be a better fit after this, though.

runners.comment: Showed ability when fourth of seven on Brighton debut; could progress
runners.spotlight: Sent off 11-2 and showed ability when fourth of seven on his Brighton debut (5f, good) last month; has AW winners in his pedigree and although progress will be needed, it is quite possible.

runners.comment: Third on his Bath debut but held next time; has possibilities if bouncing back
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when third of eight on his Bath debut (5.7f, firm) in July, but didn't back it up at Ripon (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago; closely related to an AW winner and is a possible if building on that first effort.

runners.comment: Improved from her debut when third at Lingfield 13 days ago; could get involved
runners.spotlight: Well held on last month's Newbury debut (7f, good to firm) but offered more when third of 11 at Lingfield (6f, Polytrack) 13 days ago; wouldn't need to improve much more to get involved.

runners.comment: Winning newcomers from the yard are rare, but she's still worth a market check
runners.spotlight: April foal by Belardo; dam unraced half-sister to winners Al Nefud (UAE 1m Listed) and Aldous Huxley (Listed-placed 1m2f), out of 1m2f Listed winning half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner White Moonstone; winning newcomers from the yard are rare, but she's still worth a market check.

runners.comment: Well held in two maidens in July; nurseries beckon
runners.spotlight: Well held in maidens here (7f) and at Leicester (6f, good) in July; nurseries beckon.

racecards.verdict: This can go to JUSTCALLMESERGIO who looked good on his Bath debut before contesting a Newmarket sales race.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/4 Justcallmesergio, 100/30 From The Hip, 4/1 Dandy Speed, 6/1 Iconic Dawn, 10/1 Solar Invincible, 16/1 Justmyluck, 33/1 Apex Star,  Tafah,  Ghisa, 50/1 Sorted,  Time For Action

racecards.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com 'Confined' Restricted Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.comment: Beaten around 30l on Newbury debut; transformation required but not impossible
runners.spotlight: Beaten around 30l when tenth of 13 on last month's Newbury debut (6.5f, good; 10-1); bred to be much better than that, though, and a transformation is not beyond the realms of possibility.

runners.comment: Showed more when fourth at Salisbury on his second start, but needs a another step forward
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on his Windsor debut (6f, good to firm) in July and although he showed a bit more when fourth of ten at Salisbury (6f, good to soft) last month, he will need to take another significant step forward here.

runners.comment: Showed nothing in two starts on Polytrack last month; look elsewhere
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing in two starts over 7f on Polytrack at Kempton and Chelmsford last month; look elsewhere.

runners.comment: Close third at Newcastle on second start and the form has been franked; high on list
runners.spotlight: Both starts at Newcastle, improving from his debut when beaten just over a length into third of 13 (6f) last month; the fourth horse has won twice since and that form puts him high on the list.

runners.comment: Stable has winning 2yo newcomers; worth a market check
runners.spotlight: February foal by Ardad; £18,000 yearling; dam French 1m1f winner, half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Light Infantry, out of 7.5f/1m winning half-sister to 1m Listed winner; already gelded, but stable has winning 2yo newcomers; worth a market check.

runners.comment: Interesting newcomer; stable has its share of winning 2yo newcomers; market revealing
runners.spotlight: March foal by Expert Eye; closely related to 5f/6f winner Resilience (including 2yo/AW; RPR 89), half-brother to 5f winner Expert Opinion (including AW; 78); dam 5.7f 2yo winner (85), half-sister to smart 1m/1m2f winner Sinjaari, family of top sprinter Lochsong; interesting newcomer from stable that has its share of winning 2yo newcomers; market revealing.

runners.comment: Showed some ability on his Bath debut, but may be more of a nursery type
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability when eighth of 11 on his Bath debut (5.7f, soft; 28-1) 13 days ago, but may be more of a nursery type and he has the outside draw.

runners.comment: Well held in both starts on turf last month; nurseries may look a more suitable option
runners.spotlight: 66-1 when well held over this trip at both Windsor and Newbury last month; her dam won seven times on the AW, but nurseries may look a more suitable option after this.

runners.comment: Improved from her debut when fourth at Ripon; each-way claims if stepping forward again
runners.spotlight: Green when last of five on her Newmarket debut (7f, good to soft) last month, but improved to finish fourth of nine at Ripon (6f, good) next time; each-way claims if stepping forward again.

runners.owner: C Spencer-Herbert, M Herbert & L Bellman
runners.comment: Showed promise when fourth on her Chepstow debut last month; in the mix
runners.spotlight: 11-1 and showed promise when fourth of eight on her Chepstow debut (6f, good) last month (third won next time); half-sister to a multiple AW winner and any improvement will put her in the mix.

racecards.verdict: The selection is INDEFENSIBLE whose third at Newcastle on his second start has since been boosted by the fourth horse.
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/8 Indefensible, 3/1 Melodical, 5/1 Escape Plan, 6/1 My Champion, 8/1 India Gold, 12/1 Because We Can,  Ten Carat Harry, 50/1 Reality Queen, 80/1 Delta Dan

racecards.race_name: Arc All-Weather £1Million Bonus Returns Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Not seen since showing some promise on his Southwell debut 13 months ago; watch market
runners.spotlight: Sent off 10-1 and showed some ability when fifth of 13 on his Southwell debut (6f, Tapeta) in August last year, but not seen since (gelded in the meantime); worth a market check.

runners.comment: Runner-up in two of his first three starts including at Southwell last month; major player
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two of his first three starts, the latest when beaten less than a length at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) last month (fifth has won since); major player.

runners.comment: Showed ability in both starts last year but off 16 months; worth monitoring in the market
runners.spotlight: Fifth of 11 on his York debut (6f, good) in May last year and third of six at Hamilton (6f, good) the following month, but off since (gelded in the meantime); closely related to a Tapeta winner and worth monitoring in the market; hood worn on both previous starts now removed.

runners.comment: Runner-up in both starts over this trip on Tapeta last month; major player again
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in both starts over this trip on Tapeta last month, possibly paying for early keenness when beaten at 5-6 at Newcastle on the second occasion; major player again, especially if settling better.

runners.comment: Would be a very rare winning newcomer from the stable
runners.spotlight: 1,500gns yearling; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 77), half-sister to 5f/6f winner Twilight Madness and 6f-1m winner Rocket Power, out of 5f/6f winning half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Illuminate; would be a very rare winning newcomer from the stable.

runners.comment: Tailed off in both starts on Tapeta last month
runners.spotlight: 200-1 when tailed off in both starts on Tapeta at Southwell (1m) and Newcastle (6f) last month.

runners.comment: Well beaten in her first two starts on the Southwell Tapeta; low-grade handicaps beckon
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds and well beaten in her first two starts on the Southwell Tapeta (6f/5f); low-grade handicaps beckon.

racecards.verdict: This looks to lie between GREY JAGUAR and Shamacid with slight preference for the former.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/4 Grey Jaguar, 7/4 Shamacid, 3/1 Proud To Be Fox, 8/1 Brakeman, 33/1 Washington Heir, 100/1 Yan Tahn Tethera,  Lilac Wood

racecards.race_name: Book To Stay Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap

runners.comment: A bit more is needed in order to win despite dropping into a 0-55 for the first time
runners.spotlight: Nowhere near as good as he was and although not disgraced on a few occasions on turf/Polytrack this year, more is needed in order to win despite dropping into a 0-55 for the first time; visor returns.

runners.comment: 7lb below last winning mark but has been disappointing of late; others preferred
runners.spotlight: 7lb lower than when winning a Chelmsford nursery (6f, Polytrack) last November and 5lb lower than when second of 11 at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) in May, but she has disappointed since; others preferred.

runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in March and back on a workable mark; makes plenty of appeal
runners.spotlight: Landed two 0-50 classified events over C&D in March and showed he could still mix it in handicaps when third of ten back here a fortnight ago; now back on his last winning handicap mark and makes plenty of appeal, despite wide draw.

quotes.race: Play Coral Racing-Super-Series For Free Handicap
quotes.quote: Nordic Glory was down in class and deserved to win. He just needed a bit of luck from a wide draw - Michael Attwater, trainer.

runners.comment: 1-21 and well held in both starts on turf since joining this yard; opposable
runners.spotlight: 1-21; continues to slide down the weights but well held in both starts on turf since joining this yard; opposable.

runners.comment: Has beaten just one rival in five starts since returning in February; look elsewhere
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise for Andrew Balding as a 2yo but has beaten just one rival in five starts since returning in February and finished tailed off in both outings for this yard; look elsewhere.

runners.comment: Running well at Lingfield lately and had draw excuse last time; shortlisted
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a 0-50 classified event at Lingfield (6f, Polytrack; first-time blinkers) in June; finished a close third of ten in a handicap there early this month, a wide draw may have done for her at Chelmsford (6f, Polytrack) last Thursday; shortlisted.

runners.comment: Five-time course winner; ran well a couple of times on turf in the summer; could go well
runners.spotlight: 0-15 since gaining a fifth course win over this trip in January, but twice placed at Ffos Las (6f, good/good to soft) in the summer and had a valid excuse at Chester last Friday; could go well from his good draw.

runners.owner: D Morgan And K J Parris
runners.comment: 1-25 but second in three of her last five starts; one to consider with Oisin Murphy booked
runners.spotlight: 1-25; has been racing over trips between 5f and 1m this year and came second in three of her last five starts, the latest at Chelmsford (6f, Polytrack) last Thursday; suggested a return to 7f may suit there, but still one to consider with Oisin Murphy booked.

runners.comment: Has gone close a couple of times lately, but losing run up to 24; others look more solid
runners.spotlight: Dual winner here (6f/7f) in 2020; has gone close a couple of times lately, as when beaten a short head at Newcastle (5f, Tapeta) in July, but losing run up to 24; others look more solid.

runners.comment: 1-16; in the frame in his last four starts, but not sure the return to 6f is ideal
runners.spotlight: Sole win was in a Leicester seller (6f, good) as a 2yo; in the frame in his last four starts including when fourth of 14 in a 0-50 contest at Lingfield (7f, Polytrack; first-time blinkers) last month, but not sure the return to 6f is ideal.

quotes.race: WM Installations 10th Year Anniversary Selling Stakes (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Coiled had the form to be [stable] first string, but Rotation has been working well at home, so I didn't think he should have been the big price he was.  He was too keen at Kempton so I sat nearer the front this time and chased the leaders - Mia Nicholls, rider, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: This may be more realistic on handicap debut; worth a market check
runners.spotlight: Hasn't beaten many in three maidens over 5f-7f on turf/Tapeta; this may be more realistic on handicap debut and interesting to see if there is any support for her; yard also runs Stoneacre Girl.

runners.comment: Three-time course winner, the latest off 2lb higher; each-way possibilities returned here
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has won three times here, the latest off 2lb higher over this trip last November; modest on turf lately, but each-way possibilities returned to this venue despite the widest stall.

quotes.race: totesport.com Tangerine Trees Conditions Stakes (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Savalas is just so tough and to win three in a row is some effort. I believe he is an out-and-out five furlong horse, so he will be staying at this trip. I'm not sure where we'll go next with him, we'll just take him home and have a think about it - Kevin Ryan, trainer.

runners.comment: 0-13 and has plenty to prove after five months off
runners.spotlight: The odd bit of promise for Jessica Macey, but moderate otherwise and well beaten on stable debut over C&D in April which took her record to 0-13; plenty to prove after five months off.

racecards.verdict: He could have been better drawn, but dual C&D winner NORDIC GLORY gets the vote having dropped back to his last winning handicap mark.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Sam's Hope, 5/1 Nordic Glory, 8/1 Mighty Gurkha,  Kurimu,  Sarabi,  Red Walls, 9/1 Piranha Rama,  Rotation, 12/1 Araifjan, 16/1 Havana Miss, 25/1 Savalas,  Stoneacre Girl, 33/1 Noble Phoenix

racecards.race_name: Arc £1Million Bonus Has £150K Increase! Handicap

runners.comment: Has run well over this trip and further on a few occasions this year; down in class
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler whose sole Flat success came on stable debut in a four-runner novice here (9.5f) in January; has run well over this trip and further on a few occasions in the meantime and, despite a three-month absence, is worth a second look down in class.

runners.comment: 0-24 but has shaped as though the return to this trip may suit; frame possibilities
