runners.spotlight: 375,000gns yearling; New Bay half-brother to two winners, notably Marmara Star (9.4f-1m3f including Germany/Belgium; RPR 99); dam unraced daughter of US 1m1f Grade 1 scorer; heed the market signals.

runners.comment: 650,000gns yearling; by Night Of Thunder; check the betting
runners.spotlight: 650,000gns yearling; Night Of Thunder half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Military (RPR 99), out of 7f/1m1f scorer (including 2yo/US turf Grade 3; 102); yard also runs Nova Centauri; check the betting.

runners.breeder: Macquaire Blds / Lynch Bages / Tim Hyde
runners.comment: Promising third of ten at Newbury, faring best of the newcomers; major contender
runners.spotlight: Promising third of ten at Newbury (7f, good to firm) where he was one place behind a subsequent winner, fared best of the newcomers and shaped as if this extra furlong will suit; major contender.

runners.comment: Made some late headway at Sandown and is open to improvement upped in trip
runners.spotlight: Nowhere near the winner but made some late headway for fifth of eight at Sandown (7f, good) and, in keeping with pedigree, should be suited by this longer distance; open to improvement.

runners.comment: Has plenty to find on the figures
runners.spotlight: Has plenty to find on the figures and should find nurseries more suitable after this.

runners.comment: Very weak claims on form
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who has very weak claims on form; still waiting for a handicap mark.

runners.comment: Futurity entry; eyecatching second of 13 in the Convivial Maiden at York; leading claims
runners.spotlight: 280,000gns breeze-up purchase who holds a Futurity entry; finished nicely for eyecatching second of 13, faring best of the newcomers, in the Convivial Maiden at York Ebor festival (7f, good to firm); leading claims.

runners.comment: Has frame possibilities on latest effort (AW) but needs further progress to win
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding who took a step forward with staying-on third at Lingfield (7f, AW) most recently; has frame possibilities on that form but needs further progress to get off the mark.

racecards.verdict: Convivial Maiden runner-up SPYCE is taken to go one better. Nova Centauri, a similar type, is second choice.
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/8 Spyce, 2/1 Nova Centauri, 9/2 Lighting Thunder, 7/1 Arbaawy, 10/1 Eddaari, 16/1 Royal Standard, 25/1 Wise Crack, 50/1 Blues And Royals, 100/1 Sahm Naif,  Sergeant Chaos

racecards.race_name: SSP Signs & Graphics Handicap

runners.comment: May stage a turnaround now handicapping; market support would heighten interest
runners.spotlight: Clear promise at Newmarket (7f) in sole 2yo start; disappointing twice in the spring (gelded since) but may stage a turnaround now handicapping; market confidence would heighten interest.

runners.comment: Yarmouth record is 52111; on a tougher mark now but can't be ruled out
runners.spotlight: Below par on soft ground at Epsom five days ago; in good form otherwise since June, notably winning Yarmouth races that took his course record to 52111; on a tougher mark now but can't be ruled out.

quotes.race: Download The Quinnbet App Handicap
quotes.quote: Gorgeous Mr George has been improving a lot. The first time I got on him was here when he won. We always thought he would enjoy the mile, we did the stiff seven at Carlisle last time but he didn't seem to enjoy that track - Kyle Strydom, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: May rebound provided he gets ideal ground (softer than good); on a handy mark
runners.spotlight: Bit disappointing at Ascot last time, spoiling an otherwise solid record on ground softer than good; may rebound provided he gets those ideal conditions; won off 1lb higher last autumn.

runners.comment: Ran creditably the last twice, taking record since being gelded to 231135
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably off this mark over 7f the last twice (good/AW), taking his record since being gelded to 231135; gained his wins at 6f (good to firm) but again seems likely to give his running.

runners.comment: Productive for current yard, winning four times, including latest start
runners.spotlight: Productive for current yard, winning four times, including at Lingfield (7f, AW) in June and Newmarket (6f, good to firm) latest start; climbing the weights but that reflects his general progress.

runners.comment: Largely sound record on turf; return to this sphere is the angle with him
runners.spotlight: Below form on AW the last twice; largely sound record on turf otherwise, including win as a 2yo and close third at Leicester (7f) in last attempt; return to this sphere is the angle with him.

runners.comment: May settle better and post a bigger effort back down in trip with hood on
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden who weakened in 1m handicap on soft ground most recently, having taken a good hold; may settle better and post a bigger effort back down in trip with hood applied.

runners.comment: Turf debutant who isn't particularly solid on his AW form
runners.spotlight: Turf debutant who isn't particularly solid on his AW form, having failed to back up Chelmsford maiden win; now goes in headgear on third handicap attempt; enough to prove.

runners.comment: Both wins over 6f, latest in May; not in the same form since
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 6f, latest off 1lb lower at Newmarket (good) in May; not in anything like the same form since, latterly over 7f; enough to prove.

runners.breeder: C R Withers & Mr & Mrs R Reed
runners.comment: Handicap debutant who needs to build on latest effort
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who appeared to run well at Redcar (7f) most recently but needs to build on that effort; opening mark doesn't look particularly generous.

runners.breeder: Boyce Bloodstock & Mrs C E Percival
runners.comment: Record is just 1-12 but he gained the win over C&D on penultimate outing
runners.spotlight: Record is just 1-12 but he gained the success over C&D (good) on penultimate start and enters calculations returned to this scenario; seemingly unsuited by the drop back to 6f here since.

runners.comment: Form figures of 321 since belated return to action; unexposed over 7f; respected
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 321 since his belated return to action, most recently opening his account in Lingfield AW contest on only his second attempt over 7f; unexposed at this distance; respected.

quotes.race: DragonBet Some Might Lay Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Kieran [Shoemark, jockey], very kindly, slightly held up his hands and felt he had gone a fraction too soon on Saytarr. He had to use up a lot of petrol at Southwell last time and he's had a hard race today. There's definitely wins to be had with him - Charlie Fellowes, trainer.

runners.breeder: Paul Cairns, Anthony Kirwan & Joe Burke
runners.comment: The switch to tongue-tie needs to prompt progress
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has worn various headgear since handicapping, producing form figures of 548; the switch to a tongue-tie needs to prompt progress.

racecards.verdict: Being very solid on 2025 form and unexposed at this trip, SAYTARR (nap) is preferred. Second choice is Palmarian.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Saytarr, 5/1 Tactical Plan, 11/2 Palmarian,  Gorgeous Mr George, 8/1 Thurso, 12/1 Noble Guest, 14/1 Apache Green,  My Mate Kev, 16/1 Master Of Zest, 20/1 Give Me The Night,  Big Alex Walmsley, 25/1 Blue Eclipse

racecards.race_name: Strawberry Fields Stud - We Sell Horses Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo but he has something to prove and others are preferred
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won a Windsor novice (1m, soft) last May but he's not gone on from that in only four runs since; mark continues to fall and this is a drop back in grade but he still has stamina to prove at this trip and others are preferred.

stable_tour.quote: He's been given a rating of 89, which should get us in the Golden Gates. He did well to win a novice from the front at Windsor last time and the form is working out well. He's out of a Galileo mare and I think stepping up to a mile and a quarter should suit him well. He's a horse I've always liked. 13-06-24

This lovely, big, strong colt has been doing well at home and is certainly catching my eye on the gallops. He's out of a Galileo mare and we'll start him off when the 7f maidens start in the next few weeks. 21-06-23

runners.comment: On last winning mark but was tailed off last time and he comes with risks attached
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 1m2f (AW/good to soft) for Jane Chapple-Hyam last spring; last success was off today's mark but he's finished tailed off in two of his four starts this season including at Beverley last time; comes with risks attached but he shaped with promise on his penultimate run and it would be a positive if the ground is on the slow side.

prev_owners.owner: Paul Turner and King for a Day

runners.comment: All wins have come in the spring and he's finished tailed off in last two starts
runners.spotlight: All wins have come in the spring, with one on AW but mainly on soft turf; well handicapped on his best form last season but he's failed to beat a rival in his last two runs and needs a major revival after a break.

quotes.race: Racing TV Zetland Gold Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: Arthur's Realm has appreciated the return to an easy surface and it's great that he's managed to win over a mile and a quarter - Ed Dunlop, trainer.

quotes.race: SBK Spring Mile Handicap
quotes.quote: The race fell lovely for me. Arthur's Realm travelled well and they went a nice gallop. Ed was a bit wary of the ground but as the racing has gone on they've got into it, it's a bit holding, and it suited him - Rossa Ryan, jockey

quotes.race: Follow @RiponRaces On Twitter Novice Stakes
quotes.quote: Arthur's Realm was a little awkward in the paddock but he's quite talented. We ran him over a mile and a quarter and he probably didn't quite stay: we dropped him to a mile and he's gone and won for us - Robin Trevor-Jones, assistant trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's as old as you and me put together but ran very well again in the Spring Mile a few weeks ago, finishing fifth and not beaten far - it was a race he'd won in 2022. He won the Zetland Gold Cup last year very impressively and that race has been the aim again since Doncaster, but whether the ground will come right, I don't know. He needs slow ground. He still seems to have his old enthusiasm for the game and he's been a star. 30-04-25

runners.comment: Went close on Windsor reappearance in April but he's been held there in both runs since
runners.spotlight: Record of 6-32 includes a course win and he's fairly versatile ground-wise; started this season with a near-miss at Windsor (1m2f) in April but he's finished down the field there in both subsequent runs; wasn't beaten far last time but he's not easy to predict and others are preferred.

stable_tour.quote: He has now won four of his 12 races, including last time out at Windsor earlier this month. I sent him there thinking he should be competitive but I was pleasantly surprised how easily he won. The handicapper has not been too harsh, rising him 8lb to a mark of 88 and you would hope he can win again off that. He has clearly improved from last season and this big scopey individual could have more to offer. All being well he runs next week Windsor, again over 1m2f but in a Class 3 this time. 25-05-22

prev_owners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.comment: 3yo who still has potential but he needs to find more with cheekpieces added
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 3yo; favourite in both starts for new yard last month, but after winning a Yarmouth maiden (1m2f, good to firm) he was held in sixth on handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f, soft); still has potential after only seven starts but he needs to find more with cheekpieces added.

runners.comment: On reduced mark but his last win was 12 months ago and he needs to raise his game
runners.spotlight: On reduced mark but his last win was 12 months ago and he's been beaten 5l or more in his six runs this season; ran into a well-handicapped rival when third at Sandown last time but he needs to raise his game back up in trip.

runners.comment: Gosden 3yo who still has potential but soft ground would be a concern
runners.spotlight: Gosden 3yo who made a good start in handicaps with a C&D win (good to firm) in April and creditable third at Sandown in July; still has potential but he struggled on soft at Sandown (first run since gelded) last month and he probably wants the ground to dry out.

runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo who went very close at Newmarket (1m2f); big player off unchanged mark
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden but she had a near-miss with cheekpieces added in a Newmarket handicap (1m2f, good to firm) last month; showed a good attitude with that rallying effort and she's a big player off unchanged mark.

runners.comment: Won Windsor novice in July but well held back in a handicap there last time
runners.spotlight: Off the mark (eighth attempt) when landing a Windsor novice (1m2f, good to firm) in July but that was a narrow success in small field and she couldn't hang in there on her return visit seven days later; has had only two chances in handicaps but this is a step back up in grade and others are more persuasive.

runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo and he looks interesting upped to 1m2f on handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to a US Grade 1 winner; showed some promise in his qualifying runs, including when third of 12 on Chelmsford reappearance (1m, AW) last month; made some good late headway in that race and he looks interesting now upped to 1m2f on handicap debut.

racecards.verdict: Top of the list is the lightly raced 4yo CLERMONT FERRAND, who is on the same mark as for her near-miss at Newmarket last month.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Clermont Ferrand, 3/1 Akrramm, 13/2 Keble Spirit, 15/2 Life Is Rosie, 8/1 Nepal, 10/1 Lordsbridge Blu, 14/1 Silver Gunn, 16/1 Miletus,  Primo Lara, 25/1 Arthur's Realm

racecards.race_name: Goffs Two Million Series Fillies' Handicap

runners.comment: Has failed to back up Group 3 effort; fitting of hood needs to make a difference
runners.spotlight: Won on 2yo debut then seemed to run well in Ascot Group 3 but has failed to back up that form since; first-time hood needs to make a difference in this second handicap attempt.

runners.comment: Ran well in Listed grade last time but has to overcome a 20lb rise in her mark
runners.spotlight: Improved form since dropped to 6f, winning at Salisbury then fourth in Pontefract Listed event, both on good to firm; whether she can defy 20lb rise for latter effort remains to be seen; hood removed.

runners.comment: Return to turf is the angle with her; had a solid 2yo campaign in this sphere
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame on AW in sole outing this term; solid 2yo campaign on turf featured a soft-ground win in conditions race and two placed efforts at Listed level; return to this sphere is the angle with her.

quotes.race: Kingsclere Stud Fillies' Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: It was very pleasing. We fancied Running Queen the first day at Newbury but it was a competitive race and she raced on the wing a bit. She seems to have come forward and she did it nicely. She's always stuck her head above the parapet and has come to hand early. She's always looked like an above-average filly, so it's nice to do it and it's for a new owner in the yard too. She'll hopefully have some nice targets throughout the year. I'm sure she'll get six furlongs no problem, but she's quick and we're happy for now keeping her over five - Ollie Sangster, trainer.

runners.comment: Interesting back down in trip, being 2-2 over 6f, albeit gained both wins on AW
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly; failed to get home over 7f at Thirsk ten days ago on return from a layoff; still has something to prove on turf but is interesting back down in trip, being 2-2 over 6f, both wins on AW.

stable_tour.quote: She was an impressive winner at Wolverhampton on Saturday after winning her maiden at the end of the last year. I think she'll be seen to better effect on the turf and she should climb through the ranks this summer. 07-04-25

runners.comment: No certainty to follow up her Newmarket success; yet to score above Class 4
runners.spotlight: Made all at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last time, taking record to 9-35; may remain competitive off just 2lb higher but is no certainty to follow up, having never won above Class 4.

stable_tour.quote: She was in very good form on the all-weather earlier this year, winning five times between February and May. She was fifth at Windsor last week and then sixth there again on Saturday. She may go to Brighton, where she's entered in the Bullet on Friday. 07-08-24

runners.comment: Gives the impression this step up to 6f is worth exploring; one to consider
runners.spotlight: Campaigned exclusively at 5f, winning twice as a 2yo; however, her efforts in a light 2025 campaign suggest this extra furlong is worth exploring; ran respectably at Sandown (soft) most recently; one to consider.

stable_tour.quote: She's a Blue Point filly owned by Amo Racing and is a really neat, sharp and strong juvenile. We've been delighted with her early work and although we'll learn a lot more about her in a couple of weeks, she's one we really like. 29-03-24

runners.comment: Never landed a blow at York Ebor festival; solid record (212) otherwise this term
runners.spotlight: Raced mainly at about 5f but gives the impression she'll be as effective back over 6f; never landed a blow at York Ebor festival most recently; solid results (212) otherwise this term and may do better still.

runners.comment: Hasn't shown a great deal of progress but is 1-1 at Yarmouth and could go well
runners.spotlight: Fresh contender, having raced only twice this season; hasn't shown a great deal of progress overall but is 1-1 at Yarmouth (debut success) and could go well.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I let the other two go on as they were breeze-up horses, but Spherical picked up very well. She's only got small feet so won despite the ground, and when we get summer ground she may well enjoy it more - James Doyle, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: She won her only start well in a valuable three-runner novice at Yarmouth, but I don't know what she achieved. She'd worked well going into the race, won it like a smart filly, and there's no reason for her not to take her chance in the Queen Mary. She won on good to soft at Yarmouth, but she will be better on a sounder surface. 9-06-24

runners.comment: Went close at Newmarket last time; may have further progress in her; possibilities
runners.spotlight: Went very close in fillies' handicap at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last time, taking form figures to 23152; goes back up in grade but may have further progress in her; possibilities off bottom weight.

racecards.verdict: With further progress a possibility, bottomweight CUBAN LADY gets the vote. Mademoiselle is second choice.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Cuban Lady, 5/1 Cinque Verde, 13/2 Mademoiselle,  Lady Roxby, 7/1 Orchid, 8/1 Rhyme Dust, 10/1 Running Queen, 12/1 Mandurah,  Spherical

racecards.race_name: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap

runners.comment: Three turf wins on good to firm; remained in form since Thirsk win (5f) last month
runners.spotlight: Fair form on soft but missed a race on the surface in August and three 5f turf wins all on good to firm; remained in form since Thirsk win last month, hampered at Windsor after then ran well from a wide draw on Lingfield AW since (Raneenn just ahead); bang there if coping with the ground.

stable_tour.quote: He's had a huge amount of issues but thankfully they all are behind him now. He's a very good-looking horse and I always anticipated he'd start winning races, so it wasn't a surprise to see him win last time out. He just needed his first run of the year and he got it right at Southwell. It's a lot easier to bridge a gap in sprint races than in middle-distance contests, so I think he'll run in some nice races and will continue to be competitive even up in grade. A track like Epsom would suit him very well and I could see him popping up in a nice race this season and picking up the pieces on the right day. 29-03-24

runners.comment: All three wins at 6f; not gone on from April win; creditable 4th on AW latest; needs more
runners.spotlight: All three wins at 6f, including on this course; successful handicap debut at Doncaster (good to firm; has won on good to soft) in April but hasn't gone on and was better drawn than Moulin Booj when they were fourth and fifth on Lingfield AW (5f) 12 days ago; needs more.

runners.comment: Two 6f wins on soft/good to soft; ground will suit on first go over 5f; interesting
runners.spotlight: Has run at 6f and 7f, two wins coming over the former trip on soft and good to soft; beaten a long way under a penalty in AW Racing League race in July but she'd come clear from a favourable draw at Lingfield previously; doesn't lack for pace and interesting on first 5f run.

stable_tour.quote: This filly has been a little bit behind. She won her maiden at Pontefract last year with give in the ground and I nearly had her ready for some slow ground as she was entered at Doncaster last week, but it hasn't happened. She won't run on firm ground, so we'll have to wait for the ground to change. I'd say 6f or 7f is her trip, although I think she'll stay a little bit further if needed. She's rated 74 and we'll get her going in a suitable handicap and take it from there. 30-04-25

runners.comment: Winning C&D debut then not far away in Group 2; slow start to 2025; not clear run latest
runners.spotlight: Winning C&D debut (good to firm; fair Listed run on soft) then not far away in Group 2 at Royal Ascot; slow start to 2025 but was eased after checked in his run at Newmarket (5f) last month and is on a handy mark now.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Binadham shows plenty of speed at home. He was a bit keen and he has a lot to learn but that was a good start. It will be one step at a time but, fingers crossed, he can go on to better things - Kevin Philippart de Foy, trainer, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Won this in 2023 (good to soft); only just failed back here in June; can go well fresh
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2023 (good to soft) and in fine form earlier, finding Beverley too stiff a test on penultimate start but winning the time before and only just failing to get up back here in June; can go well when fresh; likely to be involved.

quotes.race: Unison Supporting Black Workers Handicap
quotes.quote: I was a little bit concerned because Thunder Star is such a strong traveller, but she kept it going and it has opened doors as she's quite versatile with ground - Jessica Macey, trainer.

runners.comment: Well treated on close 5th in the 3yo Dash at Epsom but not at the same level since
runners.spotlight: AW winner over 6f last August; well treated on the form of his fifth of 16 in the 3yo Dash at Epsom in June but not at the same level since, including in cheekpieces (refitted) last time out; 7lb lower than his latest 5f run but others more convincing.

runners.comment: Well held on soft in 2024; two sprint wins as 3yo but lesser effort latest
runners.spotlight: Well held in two soft-ground runs as 2yo and has raced on good or quicker this year, winning over 6f and 5f; has had a good season but he was a fader at Thirsk last time and has going queries to resolve.

runners.breeder: J Kilpatrick & Mme D Ades Hazan
runners.comment: 2-27 on turf but close call before break; yard won 2 of the last 5 runnings
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner; just 2-27 on turf, most recently in 2023 (good to soft), but some good runs before a summer break included a close call at Leicester (5f, good to firm) in April and he should come on for an AW run last month; yard has won two of the last five runnings.

runners.comment: Ex-Irish; smart 5f handicap form but off since June and makes debut for new yard
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 5f Navan maiden but has smart handicap form, as when a luckless runner-up in the 3yo Dash at Epsom in 2024; capable of going well on form but lacks a recent run now making yard debut.

quotes.race: Betfred 3YO "Dash" Handicap
quotes.quote: It was Sturlasson's first time in a handicap, and normally they'd say they need a run in a handicap because it is a different type of sprinting, but he did fantastically well. He won his maiden in Navan, and the thing we were happy with it that the first few furlongs there are a bit undulating and downhill, so he might have had a bit of experience there for this. Oisin [Murphy, jockey] said the three-year-old handicap sprint at Royal Ascot is made for him - that's where he said he'd do, so all things being well, we will. When he first ran in a maiden he was second to Bucanero Fuerte, who is favourite for the Commonwealth Cup, so we knew he was pretty useful - Kieran Cotter, trainer.

quotes.race: Navan Racecourse Maiden
quotes.quote: Sturlasson's work during the week was spectacular. He skipped along there and he loved it. It was a real professional performance and I'm delighted. The lads are saying they might go for the Palace of Holyroodhouse Handicap at Royal Ascot on the Friday - Kieran Cotter, trainer.

racecards.verdict: A few with claims but BINADHAM looked to be on the way back at Newmarket recently and can take advantage of a handy mark.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Thunder Star, 4/1 Moulin Booj, 9/2 Raneenn, 7/1 Loving Apprentice, 8/1 Above, 9/1 Rogue Bullet, 10/1 Binadham,  Gold Star Hero, 12/1 Sturlasson

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.comment: Some promise in a 1m maiden at Leopardstown in July, should improve, tongue-tie fitted
runners.spotlight: Showed a degree of promise in a 1m maiden at Leopardstown in July; open to improvement; tongue-tie fitted

runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
runners.comment: In rear in a 7f Leopardstown maiden won by the stable's subsequent Moyglare winner Precise
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by Wootton Bassett to 7f 2yo/1m4f Listed winner Portland; finished towards the back in a 7f Leopardstown maiden in which today's rider partnered Sunday's Moyglare Stud Stakes winner Precise to victory; improvement more than likely.

runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit
runners.comment: Closely related to Charlie Appleby-trained 1m4f Group 3 winner Volkan Star
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; closely related to Charlie Appleby-trained 1m4f Group 3 winner Volkan Star; dam of little account, half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner Skins Game and  Listed winner Epatha.

runners.breeder: Haras Des Evees, M Daniel Cherdo & Mme Claudie Cherdo
runners.comment: Sister to French AW 2yo/US turf stakes winner Venenica, half-sister to a French winner
runners.spotlight: E21,000 yearling; fifth foal; sister to a French AW 2yo/US turf stakes winner Venenica, half-sister to a French winner Pop Life (inc AW); dam won in France; likely to need experience.

runners.comment: Never counted at Gowran; raced prominently at Tipperary but well beaten, can be ruled out
runners.spotlight: Never counted at Gowran; raced prominently at Tipperary but well beaten; can be ruled out.

runners.owner: M&T Wachman,M Duffy & M O'Reilly Hyland
runners.comment: Emerged with credit from a Group 3 attempt at the Curragh, rallied well, trip should suit
runners.spotlight: E130,000 yearling; not beaten far when fourth of eight on debut in a 7f maiden at Leopardstown; emerged with credit from an ambitious Group 3 attempt at the Curragh; lost her place three furlongs out and had plenty to do before rallying to finish fourth of six; extra furlong should suit.

runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
runners.comment: A staying-on third over 7f at the Curragh, winner Diamond Necklace has boosted the form
runners.spotlight: Market drifter at 28-1 when a staying-on third over 7f at the Curragh last month; winner Diamond Necklace boosted the form with an odds-on victory in a Listed race at Leopardstown last Saturday.

runners.comment: Some ability, opening mark of 74 indicates she will be of more interest in handicaps
runners.spotlight: Signs of winning potential; opening mark of 74 indicates she will be of more interest in handicaps

runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
runners.comment: Dubawi filly out of three-time Group 1 winner Alice Springs, could be in the mix on debut
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; Dubawi filly is a sister to Group-placed 2yo winner Prettiest, half-sister to 1m2f winner Canute; dam Alice Springs, by Galileo, a three-time Group 1 winner in Falmouth, Matron and Sun Chariot; could be in the mix on debut.

runners.comment: Half-sister to a winner in Australia, dam French winner and half-sister to Pattern winners
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to a winner in Australia; dam French 1m3f winner, half-sister to winners Shamdinan (1m2f US Grade 1) and Shahwardi (1m4f Australian Group 2); trip should be fine for debut.

racecards.verdict: Curragh third LOOKINGFORARAINBOW looks the part with the form advertised by the winner Diamond Necklace at Leopardstown last Saturday
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Lookingforarainbow, 11/4 White Sand Beach, 3/1 La Fogata, 8/1 Cameo, 10/1 Zermatt Star, 12/1 Ballaghkeen, 14/1 Chicago Moon, 20/1 Ifweonlyhavelove, 25/1 Princess Sioux, 66/1 Jewels Legacy

racecards.race_name: Punchestown Is Back Maiden

runners.comment: Unable to make any impact first time out in a 1m1f maiden at Tipperary
runners.spotlight: Unable to make any impact first time out in a 1m1f maiden at Tipperary.

runners.comment: Half-brother to a Listed winner, dam Listed winner; finished second in Naas barrier trial
runners.spotlight: 80,000gns Tattersalls Guineas Breeze Up purchase; seventh foal; half-brother to winners Twist 'N' Shake (1m inc Listed; RPR 109) and Groovy Baby (6f AW; 76); dam 1m-1m2f winner (inc Listed; 107), half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner France; second in a barrier trial.

runners.comment: Looked in need of further when the stable's second-string in a 6f maiden at the Curragh
runners.spotlight: Looked in need of further when the stable's second-string on debut in a 6f maiden at the Curragh; that was a good contest, his stablemate Puerto Rico (runner-up) won the Group 2 Champagne Stakes at Doncaster on Saturday; fourth and fifth have won since.

runners.comment: 50-1, faded from two out when last of ten on debut over 7f at the Curragh, gelded now
runners.spotlight: 50-1, faded from two out when last of ten on debut over 7f at the Curragh; has been gelded.

runners.comment: E67,000 at Breeze Up; half-brother to Listed winner Dadoozdart, in rear in barrier trial
runners.spotlight: E67,000 Tattersalls Ireland Breeze Up; eighth foal; half-brother to Listed Dadoozdart and three other winners; dam twice-raced half-sister to Dewhurst winner Intense Focus; toward the back in a Naas barrier trial.

runners.comment: Frankel colt is a brother to winners Covey, Faiyum and Rising Sign, deserves respect
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; Frankel colt is a brother to winners Covey (7f/1m inc AW), Faiyum (Group-placed 1m) and Rising Sign (7f/1m1f inc 2yo/France); dam unraced sister to 1m Listed winner Forgive, half-sister to useful 6f/7f winner Right Touch; has to be taken seriously.

runners.comment: 66-1, no show first time out in a 1m1f maiden at Tipperary, up against it
runners.spotlight: 66-1, no show first time out in a 1m1f maiden at Tipperary; up against it.

runners.owner: Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

prev_owners.owner: Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

runners.comment: Definite signs of winning potential in races over 7f and 1m at the Curragh, can feature
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica colt who cost E130,000 as a yearling; shaped reasonably well as stable's third when sixth of ten over 7f at the Curragh; short of room in the final furlong when 4l third to favourite and stablemate Hawk Mountain over 1m on his second outing.

runners.comment: Form of his Curragh second was given a good boost when the third scored readily at Gowran
runners.spotlight: Always prominent on debut over 7f at the Curragh; despite running green, he maintained his effort to finish second in a race dominated by outsiders; third-placed Take Charge Star stepped up from his luckless run to win easily at Gowran.

runners.comment: Dawn Approach filly, unraced dam out of winning sister to 6f-1m2f winner Rince Deireanach
runners.spotlight: Second foal; Dawn Approach filly, unraced dam out of 1m winning sister to 6f-1m2f winner Rince Deireanach.

racecards.verdict: Jessica Harrington's runner RESURGENCE can build on a debut second at the Curragh, form boosted by the third in winning at Gowran
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Resurgence, 5/2 Port Of Spain, 3/1 Mid Flight, 8/1 Back Straight,  Cape Cod, 14/1 Fior Bhuioch, 16/1 Kanzashi, 20/1 Admiral Will Brown, 25/1 Party With Marty, 33/1 Gold Youmzain

racecards.race_name: Nostra Supporting Laois GAA Handicap

runners.comment: Mixed form since Leopardstown win in March but penultimate Naas second makes him a player
runners.spotlight: Belatedly got off the mark over this trip at Leopardstown in March and couple of good runs since, notably clear with winner when beaten a head over 7f at Naas; six days later didn't really land a blow at Galway; can't be ruled out.

quotes.race: Legacy Wine Bar Handicap
quotes.quote: If there was ever a horse who deserved a win it's Dark Viper. He's a stable favourite and a good lead horse. He had a blow at the Curragh so we knew he would step forward fitness-wise and I think the better ground and step up in trip suited. It wouldn't surprise me if he went in again - Shane Foley, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: After failing to get his head in front in his first 19 starts, he finally did it at Leopardstown the other day. I was beginning to wonder if he ever would but thank God he did. You could run him every day of the week if you needed to. Seven furlongs or a mile, either trip is no problem to him at all. 23-04-25

runners.comment: Held since Limerick win in June, needs to bounce back; soft ground no problem
runners.spotlight: Seven wins on turf up to 1m and in varying ground conditions; not at his best in four runs since winning at Limerick in June under today's rider so needs to bounce back; acts on soft to rain no issue.

quotes.race: Oktoberfest Weekend Handicap
quotes.quote: Facethepuckout has been consistent all year and been placed five times. That's his ground. The race just worked out for him as they have gone a real good gallop which he needs over seven. Leigh rode him for luck and it's worked out - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer

quotes.race: Racing Club Membership Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Facethepuckout came out of the Curragh race well. He's just sweet and in a good vein of form at the minute. He's well handicapped on his old form but chances are the handicapper will catch up with him - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer.

quotes.race: Listowel Arms Handicap
quotes.quote: Facethepuckout was bought as a fun horse. He has sweetened up lovely and looked a different horse today. He's fit and well and went through the ground - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer.

quotes.race: Derek Ryan Live After Racing Nursery Handicap (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Donnacha [O'Brien] gave him a nice ride and on his Galway run he was entitled to be thereabouts. He'd probably be okay going a mile as well. He'll go for another nursery and I think there is more progression to come from him - Joseph O'Brien, trainer

runners.comment: Outstayed at Navan latest, this easier mile should suit
runners.spotlight: Had a busy time of it in first half of season, best efforts over 7f at the Curragh, winning off 68 and good third in premier fillies' handicap; back to similar level at Navan last time, keen enough and ultimately outstayed by a 2.5l winner over a mile; this easy mile could suit.

quotes.race: View Restaurant At Dundalk Stadium Median Auction Maiden (Div II)
quotes.quote: We didn't go very fast early. Neo Smart picked up nice into the straight. The second horse headed her but to be fair she was only getting going to the line. She handled the surface well - Rory Cleary, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Galway winner up 5lb to career-high; good chance of stay this easy mile
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on seasonal return over just shy of this trip at Roscommon in May and second career win at Galway a fortnight ago under a positive ride, battling well to hold off a favourite over 7f; tongue-tie added to blinkers worn recently and this easy mile could be within his compass.

quotes.race: Golf At Gowran Park Handicap
quotes.quote: Gangsta Man was a bit unlucky in a couple of his runs and he probably deserved that. He had a nice draw here and he kind of got the luck in running. There are no plans with him as of yet. The plan was to bring him to the horse in training sales at Newmarket, so I don't know if he will run again between now and then - Kevin Smith, trainer.

runners.comment: Not at best at Galway but previous narrow defeat at Killarney gives him definite claims
runners.spotlight: Experienced 3yo whose wins have been up to 1m2f and handles most conditions; not at best at Galway latest but previous narrow defeat at Killarney gives him definite claims.

quotes.race: Royal County Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: I thought Wesley [Joyce, jockey] was actually going a bit too fast! Everybody has been saying Sunriseontheboyne wasn't staying, but we knew he stayed. I think we were done for a little bit of toe the last day, so decided we weren't going to let that happen today. He has been coming out well out of stalls, so the plan was to make the running, kick on two-and-a-half or three furlongs down and see if we could get home. It was nice to get it and is a lovely pot at our local track - Michael Mulvany, trainer.

runners.comment: Back-to-back 7f Leopardstown wins last month; upped in grade but big player if staying
runners.spotlight: Took 12 attempts to get off the mark when scoring over 7f at Leopardstown last month and quickly doubled up in similar event a fortnight later off 7lb higher, front two clear; upped in grade here but very much a player if staying.

runners.comment: Comeback Roscommon second augurs well, especially if ground softens
runners.spotlight: Won Roscommon maiden on heavy last season and very lightly raced since, returning from five months' absence with fine second at same venue early this month, up with pace throughout when second on soft; will appreciate any softening of the ground.

quotes.race: Murray Ambulance Fillies & Mares Maiden
quotes.quote: Counterculture did it all on her own and her last 100yds were her best. She is getting sharper every run. She is a big strong mare and she gets through that sort of ground well - Willie McCreery, trainer.

runners.comment: German 1m4f winner; nothing yet for this yard
runners.spotlight: Two 1m4f wins in Germany and Listed-placed over 2m; three moderate runs this term for current yard, most recently when dropped back to today's trip at Galway.

runners.breeder: Lynch Bages, Camas Park & Summerhill B/S

prev_owners.owner: Mrs Noreen Galvin & John P Wade

runners.comment: Naas winner on yard debut but no show at Roscommon since
runners.spotlight: Roscommon claimer winner for Jack Davison and winning start for current yard in 7f Naas handicap last month, coming off the pace in beating Touted Plan (winner since) a nose; never sighted off 5lb higher at Roscommon 15 days later; more needed but meets Touted Plan on 11lb better terms here.

runners.comment: Bellewstown maiden winner hit with 11lb rise for handicap return so has it to prove
runners.spotlight: Just denied by Spent All Me Money over 7f at Naas and ran okay at Killarney before seemingly much improved back in maiden company to win over this trip at Bellewstown; hit with 11lb rise so stiff task but going the right way.

runners.breeder: Exors Of The Late Sir Robert Ogden
runners.owner: Ms Olive Walsh & Mrs Paul Shanahan
runners.comment: Excuses latest when beaten favourite; 2lb below winning mark early in season now
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been at 7f, including at Leopardstown in April; safely held since (excuses last time when favourite) but handicapper has relented and now 2lb below winning mark; interesting.

runners.comment: Initial 2yo promise for Charles Hills didn't materialise; still unexposed on yard debut
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo; placed in Newbury maiden last year but tame comeback run in Redcar handicap (7f, good-to-firm) in May before leaving Charles Hills; probably best watched on yard debut.

runners.owner: A Oliver & Blue Candi Lab London Limited
runners.comment: Blinkers tried in second handicap attempt, 5lb below his Galway run; don't rule out
runners.spotlight: Consistent form in maidens over shorter, although recent Bellewstown fourth to Touted Plan was below his best; withdrawn since on account of ground so won't want much rain; blinkers tried on second handicap attempt.

runners.comment: Fair form this year without success; handicapper giving him every chance
runners.spotlight: Wins have come at Leopardstown (1m) and Dundalk; has been running consistently without looking likely to add to his tally but is now 8lb below his last winning mark.

quotes.race: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Tickets On Sale Now Handicap
quotes.quote: Ferrybank keeps running to his mark. He likes this place, the Curragh didn't suit him the last day. He'll win another one somewhere - Ted Walsh, trainer.

quotes.race: Join Us On Our Social Channels Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Ferrybank likes to be wide and was drawn wide. He was game, pulled it out and might win another one. He gallops out well - Ted Walsh, trainer.

quotes.race: Follow Us On Twitter @DundalkStadium Maiden (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Ferrybank's form was plenty good enough to win and he probably wants seven. He broke smart from a bad draw and won well. He's a grand sound, honest horse and will win plenty of races - Ted Walsh, trainer

runners.owner: P E Ryan & J G Shanahan
runners.comment: Won on this card last year, slow starter of late so caution advised; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; won here over 1m1f on this card last year (off 60) and third career win came at Bellewstown in April; compromised by slow starts last two outings so caution advised.

quotes.race: William Hill Proper Betting Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: Rockbury Lad is a very frustrating horse. He's had a couple of fractures and he needs to be smuggled into races. We'll see what the handicapper does - Michael O'Callaghan, trainer.

runners.comment: Hasn't counted in three handicaps, reduced mark but hard to fancy; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; four runs for Jessica Harrington didn't offer much and hasn't counted in three handicaps; reduced mark but hard to fancy.

racecards.verdict: Wide open but perhaps ALTIMARA can eke out a bit more improvement now upped to a mile to complete the hat-trick from a good draw
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/2 Altimara, 13/2 Counterculture,  Enchanted Garden, 15/2 Neo Smart, 8/1 Gangsta Man, 9/1 Touted Plan, 10/1 Dark Viper,  Sunriseontheboyne,  Spent All Me Money, 12/1 Facethepuckout,  Sun Soldier, 14/1 Ferrybank, 25/1 Goin',  Evangelic

racecards.race_name: Hospitality On Sale Now Race

runners.comment: Fifth in a Listed race at Killarney since winning at the same venue, trip should suit
runners.spotlight: Improved from Curragh debut in June to beat three more experienced rivals in a 1m Killarney conditions event in July, winning going away on softish ground; left an impression he might be suited by a longer trip when fifth of nine in a steadily-run Listed race over 1m at Killarney last month.

runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & S P O'Connor
runners.comment: On a lengthy losing sequence, form of Irish Cambridgeshire fourth offers him a good chance
runners.spotlight: No further success since a 1m Curragh handicap win in August 2023; fair form on opening two runs this campaign before failing to run to expectations in a premier handicap at the Galway festival; too keen then, he was much better when visored at the Curragh last time, finishing fourth in the Irish Cambridgeshire.

quotes.race: Paddy Power Supporting Cancer Trials Ireland Irish Cambridgeshire (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Coeur d'Or is just a very tough, genuine and game horse. In his younger days he was a very immature horse but I gave him plenty of time and now it's paying dividends. He loves to come from off the pace and is very consistent. He will step up a couple of furlongs and come back here on Champions Weekend for the Northfields Handicap - Dermot Weld, trainer.

quotes.race: COLM QUINN BMW Mile Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Coeur D'or has been a very consistent all year. He is a very brave horse. We were a little worried about the ground, but this has been the plan and the horse delivered. He took a lot of time to come to hand but patience paid dividends - Dermot Weld, trainer.

quotes.race: Plusvital Speed Gene Handicap
quotes.quote: Coeur D'Or got an easy lead, they went no pace, and it worked out well. Chris kicked off the front end and he won well, it was a super ride - Dermot Weld, trainer.

quotes.race: Pride Of Place Maiden
quotes.quote: Coeur D'Or has done a few different things with him at home and we thought coming here might give him a new lease of life. He toughed it out well - Chris Hayes, rider.

runners.comment: Not a bad effort in a handicap at Galway last week, not well suited by the race conditions
runners.spotlight: Most recent of four wins came at last year's Galway festival meeting; not far off his best when chasing home an easy winner at Killarney in July; towards the rear in the Colm Quinn BMW Mile at Galway; much better at the same venue last week; not well suited by the race conditions.

runners.comment: Dual Listed winner in Germany for Andreas Suborics; might prefer further, no recent run
runners.spotlight: Dual Listed winner in Germany for Andreas Suborics, suited by 1m4f on good ground; bought by current connections for E43,000 last August; fourth in a 1m4f Leopardstown handicap on stable debut last October; ran in a Listed race on final start; down the field in a maiden hurdle at Navan in April,

runners.comment: Winning debut at Leopardstown, soft ground and inexperience found him out at Roscommon
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut in a 1m2f maiden at Leopardstown in July, travelling well into the race and quickening up nicely on good ground; a combination of soft ground and inexperience seemed to find him out when raised in class at Roscommon

quotes.race: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Maiden
quotes.quote: We knew Heiselectric was fairly useful, a couple of things happened to him as a 2yo and we didn't get a chance to get him out. He's never worked more than 6f and never on grass but everything he does is on the bridle. I was going to try him at 7f but then I thought we'd run him here, he's a big horse. - Noel Meade, trainer, speaking to Racing TV

runners.comment: Smart form in France at three, ideal opportunity at these weights if she retains ability
runners.spotlight: Smart filly in France last year; won 1m2f Group 2 Prix Saint-Alary at Chantilly early in the season; seventh in the Prix de Diane before a fine effort when second to the subsequent Group 1 winner Friendly Soul in the 1m2f Group 2 Prix Alec Head at Deauville; struggled in the Arc; never involved in a Group 2 at the Curragh in May on Irish debut; good opportunity here.

stable_tour.quote: She was a Group 2 winner in France last year and has settled in well since joining us. She's a nice filly and will probably start off in the Ridgewood Pearl Stakes on Guineas weekend at the Curragh. 24-04-25

racecards.verdict: A Group 2 winner on soft in France at three, BIRTHE should be too strong for these rivals if retaining her ability and enthusiasm
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/4 Birthe, 100/30 Coeur d'Or, 4/1 Barbizon, 6/1 Lips Freedom, 8/1 Heiselectric, 20/1 Grey Leader

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.comment: Calyx colt finished second in Leopardstown barrier trial last month
runners.spotlight: Calyx colt cost 30,000euros as a foal and 47,000euros at the breeze-ups; half-brother to six winners inc Boomshackerlacker (6f-1m; RPR 108), Girl's Hope (French 1m1f/1m2f; 90) and Miss Rainbow (5f-7f inc AW; 65); dam 7f winner (68); second in Leopardstown barrier trial last month.

runners.comment: Gleneagles gelding; worth a market check on debut
runners.spotlight: Son of Gleneagles cost 9,000euros as a yearling; closely related to Czech 1m-1m4f winner Beauty Approach (inc 2yo), half-brother to 1m AW winner Smart Deal (RPR 77); dam 7f/1m winner (inc AW; 91).

runners.comment: Plenty keen on recent handicap debut second; back in maiden company, has to settle better
runners.spotlight: Went close in Curragh maiden on 2yo debut for Ger Lyons but regressive this year until much improved on last month's handicap debut at Killarney, tearing off into clear lead under today's rider and rallying well once head to finish runner-up over 1m; returns to maiden company here and will have to settle better to see this longer trip out.

runners.comment: Well held both starts this term and awaiting a handicap mark
runners.spotlight: Fair run at Galway last October on her only start at three; well held both starts this term and awaiting a handicap mark.

runners.comment: Recent debut fourth at Gowran encouraging; longer trip could suit
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to a winner in UAE; dam maiden, half-sister to 1m2f winner Blue Symphony (dam of Group/Grade 3 winners), out of Cheveley Park winner Blue Duster; well-backed on recent Gowran debut, outpaced at a crucial stage before staying on late to finish fourth over 7f; longer trip could suit.

runners.comment: Wide draw not ideal on debut, likely all the better for the experience
runners.spotlight: Shaman gelding cost 18,000euros as a foal and 10,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to four winners inc Balance Of Power (7f AW 2yo; RPR 82), Kazzam (Italian 1m1f-11.5f inc 2yo) and Gaduli De L'Alguer (Italian 5f-1m4f inc 2yo); dam 6f/1m winner (74); wide draw not ideal, likely better for the experience.

runners.comment: Debutant an unlikely one to make an immediate impact
runners.spotlight: Arizona half-brother to 1m2f/1m3f AW winner Hunters Run (RPR 79); dam 1m2f-2m AW winner (80), half-sister to multiple 7f AW winner Circles; unlikely one to make an immediate impact.

runners.comment: Strong-finishing fifth (beaten 2.5l) over 1m2f at Navan 10 days ago; one to consider
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward from Leopardstown debut last month when strong-finishing fifth over 1m2f at Navan 10 days ago; one to consider.

runners.comment: Newcomer probably best watched unless market suggests otherwise
runners.spotlight: Elzaam half-sister to winners Stylish Performer (7f inc AW/Qatar; RPR 103) and Chaos Control (1m-1m4f AW Flat inc 2yo, 86; 2m hurdle); dam 7f turf/1m AW winner (91), half-sister to useful 1m2f-12.5f winner Bid Adieu; newcomer probably best watched unless market suggests otherwise.

prev_owners.owner: John Lavery & J P M O'Connor

runners.comment: Absent since no show on debut back in April in a stronger Cork maiden; market will guide
runners.spotlight: Dandy Man filly cost 150,000 euros as a yearling; half-sister to six winners inc New Energy (7f 2yo/Australian Group 3 1m1f; RPR 117), Victoria Regina (1m/1m1f inc AW 2yo/Italian Group 3; 105); dam French 9.5f/1m4f winner (101); never counted when well held on debut in better-class Cork median auction event in April; time off since and market likely a good guide here.

runners.comment: Two runs this summer make her a major player; cheekpieces now fitted
runners.spotlight: Promising seasonal return when second in 1m2f Navan fillies maiden in June (good) and posted similar RPR when second to an odds-on shot over 1m4f at Killarney following month, having forced it on softer ground; this looks a weaker race and big showing expected; cheekpieces tried.

stable_tour.quote: She won a barrier trial before running really well on her debut at Naas. She ran badly at the Curragh next time but there was an excuse for that as she picked up a little injury. She's back to herself now and she's got ability. She's a half-sister to Albigna. 23-04-25

runners.breeder: Robert Ryan, Brendan Quinn & Joan Quinn
runners.comment: Absent since promising handicap debut back in May; longer trip could suit, contender
runners.spotlight: Solid series of efforts over a mile in first half of the season, notably Gowran maiden second (on heavy) and final start when 6lb wrong on handicap debut at the Curragh in May, never far away third (good); time off since but step up in trip and easier ground should suit; can go well.

quotes.race: QuinnBet Spring Fillies Handicap (Premier Handicap)
quotes.quote: Lady Lilac is improving and with another year under her belt she will be a nice filly - Joseph Murphy, trainer.

runners.comment: Staying on over a mile of late; return to this longer trip to suit and one to consider
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive, notably latest two in maidens over a mile, staying on second at Bellewstown and at Navan 10 days again finished well when third; return to this longer trip to suit and one to consider.

runners.comment: Third over this trip at Gowran last month; place claims again
runners.spotlight: Improved from run to run over the summer, most recently chasing front two throughout when beaten 4.5l in steadily-run auction maiden over this trip at Gowran last month; place claims again.

runners.comment: No show on Curragh debut back in early June, can only be watched for now; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; no show on Curragh debut back in early June; can only be watched for now.

runners.comment: Ran no sort of race on last month's debut; will surely do better in time; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; Fastnet Rock half-brother to German 1,000 Guineas winner Darnation; dam unraced half-sister to 1,000 Guineas, Irish Oaks and Yorkshire Oaks winner Blue Bunting; joint-favourite but ran no sort of race on last month's Leopardstown debut; will surely do better in time.

runners.comment: Soundly beaten in both recent maidens, eligible for a mark after this; reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; soundly beaten in both recent maidens, eligible for a mark after this.

racecards.verdict: Several with similar sorts of chances but there may be more improvement in the twice-raced TAKE ME
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Take Me, 100/30 Edge Of Seventeen, 5/1 Lady Lilac, 11/2 Snapretend, 13/2 Sunlight Dance, 9/1 Playin Cool, 10/1 No Such Thing, 14/1 Dress Dance, 16/1 Galley Point, 20/1 Glenevies, 33/1 Rua Mor, 40/1 Cahir Bay, 50/1 Piatra Neamt,  Sarangpur

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.comment: Set a tough task in a Listed race at Gowran last time, could make her presence felt here
runners.spotlight: Gowran maiden winner over 1m in June; unable to make a telling impression in the Sandringham Stakes (handicap) at Royal Ascot; disappointing when last of four in a 1m1f Leopardstown handicap; set a tough task in a Listed race at Gowran last time.

quotes.race: Today's Gate Sponsored By The INPBA Fillies Maiden
quotes.quote: The draw can be a bit of a killer here as position is so important but Dancing Teapot did well to get across. I think she's a filly who can progress. She's still rated low but hopefully she can go up through the ranks. Dylan [Browne McMonagle, jockey] felt she was waiting a bit in front but she galloped to the line. She's a very good worker and has a very good pedigree. Her brother looks like a miler but she might stay a bit further - Joseph O'Brien, trainer.

runners.comment: Shaped as if she was becoming competitive again when fifth of 15 at Leopardstown last time
runners.spotlight: A very dependable performer last season in marked contrast to her profile this year; however, she has come down 16lb from a career high of 92; shaped as if she was becoming competitive again when fifth of 15  at Leopardstown last month

quotes.race: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Apprentice Handicap
quotes.quote: Presence relaxed well today. She got a good tow into the race today and did it well. She wasn't really suited by the track at Bellewstown, she wants a nice track like this one - Keithen Kennedy, rider.

runners.comment: Beat a higher-rated rival in a maiden last week, definite chance under a 7lb penalty
runners.spotlight: Second in a 1m1f Leopardstown handicap in June; failed to back that up in two Curragh runs during the summer; came good in a maiden on yielding to soft at Clonmel last week, scoring readily from an 83-rated rival on level terms; definite chance under a 7lb penalty.

runners.comment: Won over 1m at Gowran two weeks ago, has also been successful over further than this
runners.spotlight: Gowran winner on heavy in 2024; has added two more wins to her score this season over 1m2f at Roscommon in May and over 1m at Gowran a fortnight ago.

quotes.race: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Handicap
quotes.quote: Thrifty Of Digby was just ready to start back there and that's a good stepping stone to the fillies' handicap at Punchestown. We'll go there and hopefully we can have a nice autumn with her - Kate Harrington, trainer's assistant.

runners.comment: Recent form in staying handicaps does not encourage support, trip IS shorter than ideal
runners.spotlight: Six Flat wins; last success was in the valuable 1m4f Leopardstown October Handicap in 2024; recent form in staying handicaps does not encourage support; trip shorter than ideal

quotes.race: Bay Leaf Handicap
quotes.quote: Arch Enemy was always running well but we were waiting for this day. She didn't get a hard race at Leopardstown so we came here today. She got away alright today and she got the breaks today - Patrick Magee, trainer.

quotes.race: Irish Racing Yearbook 2022 Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Arch Enemy has had a few goes and has had some good runs, it has just taken time to get it together. I think she'll stay further - Mick Mulvany, trainer.

runners.comment: Won in Britain and now an import from France, handicap mark seems fair enough
runners.spotlight: Winner of a 6f maiden at Pontefract last season when trained by Ralph Beckett; had four runs in France this season, the most recent when second to an eased-down winner in a 1m2f handicap at Marseille-Borely in June; Juddmonte castoff is the first foal out of a useful mare from a good family; bought by Pat Flynn for 30,000gns; of some interest.

runners.comment: An excellent second in a 1m4f Galway festival handicap, best over further than this.
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over 1m4f at Bellewstown two years ago; has six seconds to her name, including two of her three outings this term, over 1m at the Curragh in March and an excellent effort behind a well-fancied winner in a 1m4f Galway festival handicap; best over further than this.

runners.owner: Dooley Thoroughbreds & J Fairhurst & Deepak Dhand
runners.comment: Profiting from a drop in grade to win a claimer at Gowran two weeks ago, raised 6lb
runners.spotlight: Won a Sligo nursery for Jessica Harrington last year and some good runs in defeat after; mostly below that form for this yard until profiting from a drop in grade to win a claimer at Gowran two weeks ago; 6lb rise might not be too much with her confidence restored.

quotes.race: Membership At Gowran Park Fillies & Mares Claiming Race
quotes.quote: Gloriously Glam has dropped in grade a bit and she ran well in a claimer here before. She was sulking a wee bit on us and I'd say even the bit of juice in the ground has helped her. She ran a nice race for Jessie [Harrington] up the Curragh before the boys bought her online. She loves it around the bend there. She can jump out and dictate. Adam was very good on her, he took no messing and just sort of sent her. She likes that. She sulks a wee bit in behind. She will be able to hold her own in races like this and I think she will sneak a handicap somewhere along the line - Ado McGuinness, trainer.

quotes.race: Guinness Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Gloriously Glam got it easy in front which helps around here. She handled the ground well - Shane Foley, rider.

runners.owner: Mrs J S Bolger & James Peter Dowling
runners.comment: Won C&D maiden a year ago, started the season brightly, has failed to build on that so far
runners.spotlight: Won C&D maiden a year ago. made a good start to the season when second over 1m2f at Leopardstown; Tipperary run in April was too bad to be true; better when seventh of 18 at Leopardstown in June, though not quite up to scratch since she was sent off favourite.

runners.comment: Won a weak maiden, did not seem to stay when beaten 10l in fourth over 1m4f at Roscommon
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form in July to run away with a weak Ballinrobe maiden over just short of 1m2f; did not seem to stay when beaten 10l in fourth over 1m4f at Roscommon, fading from over a furlong down.

runners.comment: Won a 1m soft-ground Limerick maiden in April, down the field in Galway festival handicap
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m soft-ground Limerick maiden in April; down the field in Galway festival handicap on her only start since then; Narlita finished second in that event.

runners.comment: Back to form with a 40-1 victory over an extended 1m at Galway last week, raised 10lb
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Leopardstown in July; next three outings were well short of that standard; returned to form in style with an emphatic 40-1 victory over an extended 1m at Galway last week; raised 10lb for that.

runners.comment: Seldom too far away, fourth in a 19-runner maiden at Naas last month offers some hope
runners.spotlight: Has reached the first three in three of her 12 races and fourth on four occasions; needs to find a bit extra, but her fourth in a 19-runner maiden at Naas last month offers some hope; winner Noli Timere subsequently won a Navan handicap from Neo Smart

runners.comment: Disappointing in her last three races given the promise she showed on debut last year
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in her last three races given the promise she showed on debut last year; has had a break; perhaps premature to write off, but hard to be confident.

runners.comment: First reserve, decent run at the Curragh before Down Royal second, shortlist if running
runners.spotlight: Won 1m Bellewstown claimer in July, after which he left Dick Brabazon; good run on second outing for this stable, finishing fifth in a 21-runner contest over 1m at the Curragh; another solid display when second over a longer trip at Down Royal 11 days ago; first reserve.

runners.comment: Second reserve, promise on her only start at two, hard to assess on her two 2025 outings
runners.spotlight: Promise on her only start at two; hard to assess on her two 2025 outings; second reserve.

runners.comment: Third reserve, three-time hurdles winner, only three runs on the Flat, others preferred
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner, only three runs on the Flat, others preferred; third reserve.

quotes.race: Sign Up & Save With Fairyhouse.ie Mares Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: We were confident coming here today. Katherine is an unbelievable jumper and that's a massive help to her. She's been tricky to train and there have been little things we've started to figure out with her - Harry Sexton, jockey.

quotes.race: TipperaryRaces.ie Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Katherine had some good runs in bumpers but we always thought she would be better over hurdles. We like her a lot and we did fancy her today and she handled it really well today. She jumps really well - Harry Sexton, rider.

racecards.verdict: Having bounced back to form by beating higher-rated rivals in a maiden at Clonmel IT'S ALL PEACHY (nap) get the vote to defy a penalty
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Its All Peachy, 6/1 Thrifty Of Digby, 13/2 Electric Beauty, 7/1 Dancing Teapot,  Adrienne, 8/1 Gloriously Glam, 10/1 Presence,  Ardeur,  Narlita,  Ard Na Mara, 12/1 Kashel Spring, 14/1 Victoria Kesia,  Lily Parr, 16/1 Arch Enemy

racecards.race_name: See You On The 14th & 15th October Handicap (Div I)

runners.comment: Slow starts of late; returns to longer trip and drops in grade but hard to be confident in
runners.spotlight: All five wins in Britain came on AW at 1m-1m2f; best run for current yard was slow-starting Naas second (1m, yielding), even more slowly away at same venue latest when soundly beaten; returns to longer trip and drops in grade but hard to be confident in.

stable_tour.quote: He was bought relatively cheaply out of David Menuisier's yard and we got him to win his first two races for us, both at Lingfield over 1m2f. He has since run third twice and then eighth at Chelmsford. He may go back there on Thursday because that run wasn't him at his best. He's progressive and his work at home has been good. He'll go well on turf this summer, on good or even quicker ground. 10-05-23

runners.comment: Well held since Gowran win and not sure this step up in trip is a positive
runners.spotlight: Heavily-raced 8yo was scoring for the seventh time when winning over a mile at Gowran in May; well held since however and not sure this step up in trip is a positive.

quotes.race: Barney Curley Charity Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: Mogwli took me there once I was able to take my time on him. Once I got past the horse in front he was able to pick up - Wesley Joyce, rider

quotes.race: NavanRacecourse.ie Handicap
quotes.quote: Mogwli broke well, there is lovely fresh ground on that rail and Jack just kept him happy. The horse fought them off well too - Willie McCreery, trainer.

quotes.race: TRM Hoofmaker Handicap
quotes.quote: Mogwli doesn't like the wind and having it behind him was the best thing for him. He rode a grand race and I loved the way he fought back at the finish - Willie McCreery, trainer.

quotes.race: Fairyhouse.ie Handicap
quotes.quote: Mogwli was bred by my wife. He's a tough and hardy little fellow and he loves racing. He'll be kept on the go for the apprentices to ride - Willie McCreery, trainer

runners.comment: Best form at 1m, this easy track gives him a chance of staying but others preferred
runners.spotlight: Naas success over a mile in July was the furthest he's won over; didn't appear to stay 1m2f at Navan latest; this easier track gives him a chance of staying but others preferred.

quotes.race: Complete Coverage Of Irish Racing Only On Racing TV Claiming Race
quotes.quote: Roderick hurt himself last time at Dundalk but he has been straightforward otherwise. He's a fine big horse, he's not a Dundalk horse, and he could be more one for hurdles - James McAuley, trainer

quotes.race: Racing UK EBF Novice Stakes
quotes.quote: Roderick did get a little outpaced but he finished off his race well and that is the main thing. It is clear that he needs six furlongs now - Tony Hamilton, rider.

quotes.race: Edinburgh Gin EBF Stallions Novice Stakes
quotes.quote: He was a bit slow out of the gates but he was relaxed through the race and had no problem coming through the field. He will be better of over a bit further in time - jockey Connor Murtagh

runners.comment: Good Clonmel second at this trip 5 days ago; major player off same mark
runners.spotlight: Maiden knocking on the door on AW last year and again on turf this summer, most recently making much of running over this trip at Clonmel five days ago when beaten 0.75l; quick return off same mark and seems sure to go close with rider now claiming 7lb although wide draw not ideal.

runners.comment: Stiff 1m2f at Navan found her out but respected back on an easier track here
runners.spotlight: Early Dundalk form was quite promising and pick of turf form came off reduced mark when third over just short of this trip at Tipperary last month (yielding); stiff 1m2f at Navan found her out five days later but respected back on an easier track here.

runners.comment: Upped in trip here off 4lb lower and remains unexposed so don't rule out
runners.spotlight: Middling runs in three maidens and never dangerous on handicap debut over 7f at Gowran last time; upped in trip here off 4lb lower and remains unexposed so don't rule out.

runners.comment: Recent comeback run at Tipperary mildly encouraging but more needed
runners.spotlight: Recent comeback run at Tipperary mildly encouraging but more needed.

runners.comment: Nearly two years without a win; new yard and handicapper giving him every chance
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 1m on AW and turf in 2023; safely held in three runs this year and has since joined current yard; handicapper giving him every chance (10lb below last winning mark).

runners.comment: Hard to make a case for on recent form despite reduced mark; hood now discarded
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 11 starts and hard to make a case for on recent form despite reduced mark; hood worn last time now discarded.

runners.comment: Maiden after nine starts and continues to struggle in handicaps (various trips)
runners.spotlight: Maiden after nine starts and continues to struggle in handicaps (various trips).

runners.comment: Stiff task at Laytown latest, mildly encouraging handicap run previously
runners.spotlight: Best efforts have come over 1m; best run for new yard when fifth of 10 (beaten 5l) over extended 1m2f at Sligo, trip inadequate at Laytown latest in maiden company.

runners.comment: Glimpse of promise last time but remains hard to fancy
runners.spotlight: Moderate mare continues to slide down the ratings and has reached basement level here; glimpse of promise last time but remains hard to fancy.

runners.comment: Has to improve on recent Tipperary run over this sort of trip
runners.spotlight: Encouraging Gowran run (7f) over the summer but couldn't back it up over this sort of trip at Tipperary when tried hooded; stamina concerns; 2lb wrong.

runners.comment: Back from a break with cheekpieces tried but very hard to have faith in
runners.spotlight: Opening handicap efforts have been poor; back from a break with cheekpieces tried but very hard to have faith in; 2lb wrong.

runners.comment: Penultimate Bellewstown second gives him claims; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; latest of three wins came at Killarney last year off 53 (1m, yielding to soft); good second at Bellewstown last month on second start for the yard, didn't appear to take to the beach at Laytown latest; back up in trip here.

runners.breeder: Al Shira'Aa Farms S A R L
runners.comment: All three wins have come on soft or worse; recent form is not encouraging; reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; all three wins have come on soft or worse; recent form is not encouraging.

quotes.race: Guarantee Available On Course & Tote.ie Handicap
quotes.quote: Making the running seems to work for Don't Do Dramas. We would try him over a mile and a half again. It's a good stiff uphill finish here and he wasn't stopping. He handles soft ground well but I think he'll go on most ground, he mightn't want it firm - Mick Mulvany, trainer.

quotes.race: Golf At Gowran Park Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: That was probably Dont Do Dramas ideal conditions out there. He would probably get a little bit further in time. He is learning on the job and seems to be improving. Billy said he was a willing partner and really put his shoulder to the wheel. There is a nursery coming up in Galway for him that we won last year with Duke Of Leggagh - Mick Mulvany, trainer.

prev_owners.owner: Brian Ellison Racing Club & P Boyle

runners.comment: Down Royal winner for Pat Martin last year, yet to hit form this year; reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; four wins at Newcastle when trained by Brian Ellison; useful form for Pat Martin last year including Down Royal win; yet to hit form this year.

quotes.race: Boost Your Acca-Fenwa With BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: Explorers Way is a quirky bugger but he's loved by everyone at home and I think that helps him. Ben (Robinson, jockey) knows him and gave him a great ride and while it wasn't the best race, it's great he's won three times - Brian Ellison, trainer.

racecards.verdict: Not great strength in depth and perhaps a good opportunity for last week's Clonmel second WARAZAM to gain a belated first win
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/1 Warazam, 11/2 Sottsands, 7/1 Roderick,  Fleetfootsoldier, 8/1 Tero d'Rosa, 12/1 Goldsmith,  Mogwli,  Rock Basher, 14/1 Lady Delila, 16/1 Reponse Finale,  Churchill Gale,  Shuyak, 20/1 Sea View Lady,  The Black Stuff

racecards.race_name: See You On The 14th & 15th October Handicap (Div II)

runners.comment: Has struggled to make an impact in handicaps; first run in this grade
runners.spotlight: Won Naas maiden over 1m last summer but has struggled to make an impact in handicaps over various trips; first run in this grade but still others preferred.

quotes.race: ALTO EQUINE Building Solutions Fillies Maiden
quotes.quote: We always thought a bit of Super Exceed and she's only learning. She had the maestro on board and I was delighted to have him. I love his experience and his way with fillies. He was impressed with her because she was too far back and she was wheel-spinning on the ground there a bit - Tom Mullins, trainer.

runners.comment: Better on AW; better for recent comeback run on turf but hard to be confident in
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins up to 1m2.5f but is 0-8 on turf, entitled to come on from recent Gowran comeback run but hard to be confident in.

runners.comment: Struggled in handicaps; now 19lb below her initial mark but remains unproven over this far
runners.spotlight: Fairyhouse maiden winner this time last year but struggled in handicaps; now 19lb below her initial mark but remains unproven over this far.

runners.comment: Possibility on early-season form now off this reduced mark
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins last year were in claimers; placed twice in handicaps in May but below-par of late; not one to rule out though off this reduced mark.

runners.comment: Placed over this sort of trip at Pontefract for Ed Bethell, yard debut at Navan was poor
runners.spotlight: Placed over this sort of trip at Pontefract in June for Ed Bethell, yard debut at Navan recently was poor; others with stronger claims.

runners.comment: Winner over 1m last year for Alan King; nothing yet for current yard and back up in trip
runners.spotlight: Salisbury winner over 1m last year for Alan King; nothing yet for current yard and back up in trip here.

runners.comment: Knocking on the door of late in Gowran handicaps; big player with trip/ground to suit
runners.spotlight: An 18-race maiden but knocking on the door of late, twice placed over this trip at Gowran and most recently strong-finishing second over 1m at same track from a wide draw; seems sure to go well again.

runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo entering handicap company and upped in trip; market could prove informative
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo entering handicap company and stepping up in trip after three early-season runs; potential improver with hood back on and market could prove informative.

runners.comment: Possibly didn't stay 1m6f at Navan; back in trip here with cheekpieces tried
runners.spotlight: Sligo (1m6f) third a step in the right direction but but possibly didn't stay 1m6f at Navan; back in trip here with cheekpieces tried.

runners.comment: Back from hurdling and yet to hit form; career-low mark so not one to rule out
runners.spotlight: Two wins include 1m2f Navan success two years ago (yielding); back from hurdling and yet to hit form; career-low mark so not one to rule out.

runners.breeder: Mr J B A Al Attiyah
runners.owner: Riverstown Racing Syndicate/N O'Hare/E Hughes/K Cromwell
runners.comment: Recent Tipperary second over this trip; wide draw but big player nonetheless
runners.spotlight: Nicely improved on second handicap run at Tipperary last month (1m1f, yielding), staying on well into second late on after racing away from the pace on the near side, having hung left; not a good draw but progressive and likely big player.

runners.comment: Regressive in handicaps and now 15lb below opening mark; weak finisher but can't dismiss
runners.spotlight: Regressive in handicaps and now 15lb below opening mark; weak finisher but can't be ruled out.

runners.comment: Clear promise as a 2yo but form this year not at same level, over various trips
runners.spotlight: Clear promise as a 2yo but form this year not at the same level, tried over various trips; hard to fancy.

runners.comment: Last week's Clonmel second over the trip has to be respected off same mark
runners.spotlight: Has shaped well on a couple of occasions off lowly mark, latest when second over this trip at Clonmel last week (soft); shortlisted off same mark.

runners.comment: Off for two years until July, mild promise at Down Royal latest; reserve
runners.spotlight: First reserve; four-time winner (three in France), sole win here was on the AW; off for the guts of two years until July, mild promise on latest of three runs when not clearest of runs at Down Royal.

runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo huge prices in 3 maidens, mild promise last two; more needed; reserve
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; lightly raced 3yo huge prices in three maiden runs, mild promise last two; more needed.

runners.comment: Needs to improve for step up in trip on handicap debut to feature; reserve
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; having only fourth start, needs to improve for step up in trip on handicap debut to feature.

racecards.verdict: Having not helped her cause when hanging left in finishing second at Tipperary last month MS OLIVIA could now prove the one to beat
